title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
description: This position is working 16 hours per week, any 2 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

title: Customer Service Advisor
description: This role is working 15 hours per week, any 2 in 6 days The role of our Customer Services Advisor is critical to the success of our business. Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. The defining characteristics of our Customer Services Advisors are that they’re organised and efficient. However, apart from the technical skills that are required with the role, the most important attributes are that we demonstrate empathy, patience, kindness, friendliness, care and warmth to all our customers. The role involves the taking of customer orders and also the making of outbound courtesy calls. These calls are particularly important as many customers live alone and the phone calls they receive are often the only contact they may have in a single day. As such, many customers become very attached to their “friends” at Wiltshire Farm Foods. Wiltshire Farm Foods supplies frozen meal solutions direct to the consumer. We have a cold store to accommodate the stock necessary to be able to be able to deliver to our customers each week. The ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceed expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

title: Ingénieur Sécurité Cloud GCP ou AWS F/H
description: Dans le cadre de la mise en place d’un Centre de Compétences « Cloud Center of Excellence » pour notre client, nous recherchons un(e) Ingénieur Sécurité Cloud. Vous serez amenés à intervenir sur les sujets suivants : - Accompagner les équipes informatiques dans les pratiques de « sécurité dès la conception », visant à mettre en œuvre des politiques et des modèles de sécurité tout au long du cycle de vie des applications, de la conception à l'exécution • Maîtriser et mettre en place les principes du « Zero Trust Architecture ». • Maîtriser les services AWS ou GCP relatifs à la sécurité. • Garantir la prise de décisions de sécurité judicieuses, conformément aux politiques de l'entreprise. • Etre certifié o AWS Security speciality o ou GCP Security Engineer Ce poste situé à Guyancourt (78) Chez Orange, seules vos compétences comptent : Quel que soit votre âge, genre, origine, religion, orientation sexuelle, neuroatypie, handicap ou apparence, nous encourageons la diversité au sein de nos équipes car c’est une force pour le collectif et un vecteur d’innovation Nous sommes une entreprise handi-accueillante : n'hésitez pas à nous faire part de vos besoins spécifiques Orange obtient la certification Top Employer Global 2021. Cette certification consacre les meilleures politiques et pratiques en termes de programmes de ressources humaines. Le Groupe est certifié « Top Employer » depuis maintenant 12 ans.

title: Business Manager Performance Energétique F/H
description: Notre entité, intégrée au sein d’Orange Business, dispose de compétences pointues et reconnues dans l'intégration de solutions globales en Connectivité (LAN/WIFI/WAN/SDWAN/Data Center/MPN), Workplace (O365/Teams, Cloud Telephony, Digital Building, Mobilité) & IoT auprès de clients grands comptes et entreprises. Son rôle est de simplifier la vie digitale de ses clients, en leur offrant les meilleures expériences collaboratives tout en garantissant la meilleure connectivité grâce à des offres sur mesure. La Practice Performance Energétique et Développement Durable a pour mission de développer des offres de services et de technologies destinées à optimiser les consommations d’énergie et réduire l’empreinte carbone de ses clients. Missions : Les missions confiées au Business Manager sont organisées autour de trois grands axes : les actions commerciales, l’expertise et l’animation marketing. Définir et déployer les actions commerciales Identifier des cibles clients prioritaires et établir des listes de prospects Définir les méthodes et les objectifs des plans de prospection Contribuer activement à ces plans par des actions de prospection directe Identifier et animer les relais commerciaux internes (KAM, AM, BM, etc.) Alimenter et mettre à jour les outils de suivi commercial (CRM, fichier de suivi pipe) et assurer le reporting auprès de la direction et des parties prenantes Développer et porter l’expertise métier Apporter l’expertise métier tant en interne qu’auprès des prospects et clients Préparer et participer aux rendez-vous organisés par les commerciaux pour aider à qualifier le besoin et les réponses adéquates Assurer une veille globale (concurrence, marché, réglementation, acteurs, etc.) Détection d'opportunité de nouvelles solutions à intégrer dans le portefeuille de solutions ou partenariats à développer Définir les actions marketing et appuyer leur réalisation Appui à la réalisation d'outils de communication (supports marketing et commercial, références clients, campagne e-mailing, etc.) Proposition et appui à la réalisation de campagnes marketing opérationnel Aide à la préparation et participation aux salons d'affaires, webinaires et évènements divers Le Business Manager est assisté dans sa mission par 2 alternants qui l’appuient sur les plans d’action commerciaux et marketing.

title: ALTERNANCE - Technicien Modalités F/H
description: Vos missions : Assurer les interventions techniques préventives et curatives sur nos DM Maintenances curatives : recherche de panne sur équipement / diagnostic, remplacement de pièces détachées sur les équipements de type suivants : table de radiologie (tube, accessoires…), mammographe (tube, capteur, système de compression, système de refroidissement…), panoramique dentaire (générateur, carte électronique, faisceau de câble, serre tempe), Conebeam dentaire (générateur, tube, carte micro), ostéodensitométrie (capteur). Purge et installation de logiciels sur les consoles dédiées, préparation de nouveaux postes informatiques Maintenances préventives : révision mécanique (graissage, dépoussiérage), étalonnage et calibration de capteurs. Back up de configuration informatique, mise à jour logiciel. Installation d’équipement de radiologie : panoramiques dentaire et conebeam principalement. Fixation murale et au sol de l’équipement, assemblage installation et paramétrage. Configuration informatique pour environnement du Client.

title: Digital Marketing Manager
description: Fixed term contract for 12 months Join our rapidly growing business in a role as Digital Marketing Manager (FTC) focusing primarily on the digital marketing and paid digital strategies to support B2B’s commercial growth, as well as managing the apetito.co.uk website strategy. As a digital marketing specialist, your role will be pivotal in enhancing our online presence. Reporting to the Head of Proposition & Marketing, you will play a key role in contributing to the company’s ambitious growth targets and driving our online brand visibility. This is a fantastic opportunity for a collaborative, analytical and creative marketeer to work within a supportive, experienced and passionate team. This role is based in Trowbridge and you will be required to work on-site at least 3 days per week.

title: Technicien de production H/F
description: Vous êtes passionné(e) par la cybersécurité et souhaitez évoluer dans un environnement innovant en pleine expansion ? En mission interne et dans le cadre de l'expansion du Global Security Operations Center (GSOC) du groupe Orange, vous interviendrez au sein de la Direction de la Sécurité (DSEC) pour piloter des projets d'intégration de solutions de cybersécurité. Votre rôle sera essentiel pour assurer la supervision et la protection des actifs critiques du groupe. Vos missions : Piloter des projets d’intégration de solutions de cybersécurité : suivre le déploiement des solutions de sécurité et/ou mise en place de la supervision. Collaborer avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires : travailler main dans la main avec nos experts techniques, d'autres entités du groupe, équipes d’intégration et d’exploitation ainsi que celles du GSOC pour garantir la mise en œuvre des solutions adaptées. Évaluer et synthétiser les besoins : recueillir les besoins et synthétiser l'information, rédiger des comptes-rendus, élaborer des plannings, préparer des présentations pour le comité de pilotage, réaliser des schémas d’architecture haut niveau.

title: Ingénieur Support Sécurité H/F
description: Orange International Networks Infrastructures & Services (OINIS) est une direction technique du Groupe Orange, déployée à l’échelle mondiale avec plus de 1400 collaborateurs hautement qualifiés. Notre mission est de concevoir, planifier, maintenir et sécuriser les réseaux internationaux et services associés pour l’ensemble du groupe. Créée en 2021, OINIS SEC a pour objectif de protéger l’ensemble des infrastructures d’OINIS, depuis l’ingénierie des solutions jusqu’à la supervision de sécurité, en passant par le déploiement. Elle se structure autour de deux pôles principaux : expertise/anticipation et développement/support. En intégrant l’équipe ENG (Engineering), composée de 12 collaborateurs répartis en France, Maroc et Inde, vos missions principales seont les suivantes : Définition, l’industrialisation et le support des outils de sécurité (contrôle d’accès, authentification, gestion des vulnérabilités, supervision). Déploiement et le maintien en conditions opérationnelles et de sécurité des équipements réseaux. Mise en place des règles de filtrage Firewall. Conception d’architectures et solutions pour la protection des infrastructures, en comprenant les besoins clients et en sélectionnant les composants adaptés. Collaboration étroite avec les équipes Network et TelcoCloud pour le déploiement et la gestion des solutions. Promotion des solutions open source, tout en utilisant des produits commerciaux si nécessaire. Rédaction de documents d’architecture, guides de bonnes pratiques et référentiels. Production, configuration et déploiement des équipements réseaux IP. Support aux équipes opérationnelles pour la gestion et la résolution des incidents. Intégration de l’automatisation dans le déploiement des solutions de sécurité. Gestion de projets pour déployer les solutions dans le respect des plannings et contraintes financières.

title: Expert IoT et Réseaux H/F
description: Rejoignez une équipe dynamique au sein d'Orange, leader dans le domaine des technologies de communication. Nous sommes engagés à offrir des solutions innovantes et responsables qui répondent aux besoins de nos clients dans un monde en constante évolution. Notre culture d'entreprise valorise la collaboration, la créativité et l'exploration, tout en plaçant l'humain au cœur de nos préoccupations. Prêt(e) à connecter le futur ? Rejoignez-nous et devenez l'architecte des solutions IoT ! Description des missions : Vous concevrez et développerez des solutions IoT innovantes en intégrant des technologies réseau avancées. Vous intégrerez des modules logiciels de partenaires et fournisseurs tiers pour enrichir nos solutions. Vous rédigerez des spécifications techniques et fonctionnelles claires et précises pour guider le développement et/ou réaliserez des mission de conseil ou d’audit auprès de nos clients. Vous contribuerez au développement de bout en bout d’applications (front et back) et à leur test déploiement chez nos clients industriels. Vous accompagnerez nos clients dans la validation métier de la solution, en veillant à son bon fonctionnement dans l'environnement de production. Vous assurerez le suivi et l'optimisation des performances des solutions déployées sur le terrain. Vous participerez à des études et projets d'innovation pour anticiper les évolutions du marché et des technologies. Vous serez en charge de la formation et du support technique pour les équipes internes et les clients. Vous collaborerez avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires pour définir et mettre en œuvre des architectures IoT robustes. Vous apporterez votre expertise en support à l'avant-vente pour aider à la définition des besoins clients et à la proposition de solutions adaptées.

title: Ingénieur Devops
description: Intégré à une équipe de 21 personnes composés d'ingénieurs DevOps, Ingénieurs SecOps, Administrateurs Systèmes et de Chefs de projet ; vos missions seront les suivantes : Participer aux travaux BUILD/RUN sur le socle H2S : Gestion de l’obsolescence des composants, couche OS (TalosOS), composants Kube (Secret reloader, Cilium, ….) et applicatifs (ArgoCD, Prometheus, Graylog, ….) Evolution de l’architecture sur les nouvelles fonctionnalités requises Mettre en place de la CI/CD pour nos produits métier et nos services socles. Rédaction des pipelines, intégration outils tiers comme pour le scan sécurité, amélioration des templates, debug de l’existant Participer aux travaux de BUILD/RUN sur le tooling DevOps associé notamment Gitlab, Runners, Repositories,…. Gestion des runners et leur automatisation en IAC MCO/MCS Gitlab et repositories, AWX, …. Accompagner les équipes de développement à l’intégration de leurs applications sur nos socles en particulier H2S : charts Helm, ArgoCD, playbook ansible Co-écriture des pipelines CI/CD et playbook ansible avec les équipes de dev, intégration ArgoCD et Kubernetes ou sur infrastructure legacy, assistance à la création d’images spécifiques à partir du catalogue Participer aux guidelines/Patterns DevOps : templates CI/Charts, bonnes pratiques, security by design… Amélioration de la base de templates CI/CD Formation des devs/lead devs Participation aux travaux d’architecture R&D des produits et services

title: Ingénieur Qualité et Affaires Règlementaires
description: Assurer le rôle de PCVRR (personne chargée de veiller au respect de la réglementation) Assurer le rôle de correspondant local de matériovigilance (CLMV) Assurer le rôle de Référent Qualité pour les processus opérationnels Assurer la veille normative et règlementaire et la réponse aux exigences de notre organisation Assurer la diffusion du SMI (Système de Management Intégré) dans l’intranet de l’entreprise

title: Ingénieur Support Cloud Infrastructures F/H
description: Et si les systèmes informatiques complexes et critiques de nos clients étaient votre nouveau terrain de jeu ? Nos clients sont des clients basés en France (banques et assurances, secteur public, domaine de la santé…). Ils attendent de nous un fonctionnement de service optimal pour leurs applications critiques : appels d’urgence, dossier patient numérique, paiements sécurisés entre autres. Votre rôle : déployer et assurer l’exploitation de ces systèmes, sous la responsabilité du responsable d'exploitation. Mener à bien le déploiement des nouveaux systèmes (BUILD) en relation étroite avec les chefs de projets client et nos architectes. Assurer le maintien en condition opérationnelle et de sécurité des systèmes (RUN), assurer la gestion des incidents et des changements Participer à l’amélioration continue des services : mettre en place du monitoring fiable permettant d’anticiper les incidents. Travailler avec les technologies d’IA, de BigData et de Machine Learning afin de rendre le service toujours plus pro-actif. Intégrer une posture DevOPs afin d’automatiser les tâches d’exploitation courantes ou critiques. Rejoignez notre équipe de déploiement et d’exploitation. Cette équipe, responsable de la « Gestion des Incidents » et de la « Gestion du Changement » sur ces infrastructures est basée en France. Elle travaille en étroite collaboration avec les équipes techniques de nos clients et avec les autres équipes d’Orange Cloud for Business, situées dans nos Majors Services Centers, en Inde, Égypte, Ile Maurice

title: ALTERNANCE - Consultant Interopérabilité F/H
description: Vos missions : Acquisition des compétences propres sur nos solutions Intervention sur des projets simples/complexes pilotées par CDP Livablre attendus : Respect des process internes, production de documentations d’exploitation et de spécifications, reporting, ...

title: Service Delivery Officer F/H
description: Vos missions : Coordonner en interne les contrats de Services Managés, en back office. Assurer la prise en charge des contrats de Services Managés avec gouvernance limité. Assurer une assistance interne aux SDM et RSC Pour les contrats, en back office Prendre en charge les nouveaux contrats Coordonner les services internes pour la prise en charge d’un nouveau contrat S’assurer par des contrôles périodiques tout au long du contrat du respect du P&L, de la facturation, des charges, de la mise a jour des documents nécessaires au pilotage administratif de l’affaire, alerter sur les dates de fin de contrat. (non exhaustif) Escalader les écarts au management et proposer des axes d’amélioration et des plans d’action concret S’assurer périodiquement de la satisfaction client par les moyens adaptés à la taille du contrat Être un point de contact interne pour les questions et réclamations liées à l’affaire. Pour les contrats avec gouvernance et frontal client limité Idem contrat géré en back office + S’assurer par des contrôles périodiques tout au long du contrat que les prestations et engagements contractuels sont honorés Être un point de contact à l’écoute du client pour les questions et réclamations liées aux engagements contractuels et à la qualité de service Participation et intervention possible aux instances de comitologie. Elaboration et suivi du P&L des affaires confiées. En assistance aux SDM/RSC Sous les directives des SDM et RSC, assurer un soutien ; élaborer et faire le suivi du P&L des affaires confiées.

title: Coordinateur de projet(s) F/H
description: Vos missions : Vous serez entouré d’une équipe d’ingénieurs de production. Vous serez le garant du pilotage du ou des projets dont vous aurez la charge (budget, périmètre, planning, qualité). Interlocuteur privilégié de l’ensemble des parties prenantes, vous serez responsable de la réalisation, du bon déroulement du projet et de la satisfaction client. Vos activités principales sont de : Garantir la conduite de projets et s’assurer que tous les paramètres de la gestion du projet sont intégrés (méthodologie, ressources, délais …) Communiquer auprès de l’ensemble des parties prenantes tout au long du projet et alerter si nécessaire

title: Ingénieur commercial - Web Agency F/H
description: Les équipes d’Orange Business / Digital Services France repoussent les frontières d’Internet et innovent pour construire et optimiser le web d’aujourd’hui et de demain depuis plus de 20 ans. La Fabrique entité d’Orange Business France est une agence digitale leader de 200 personnes experte en Développement d’Applications, Digital Marketing et Hébergement. Dans le cadre de notre développement, nous recrutons un(e) ingénieur(e) commercial(e) basé(e) à Lyon pour commercialiser nos offres Web Agency. Vous êtes chargé(e) de porter et de commercialiser les offres Web Agency et de participer activement à leur développement au travers de vos relations avec les clients, prospects et les partenaires. Missions : Contribuer à la construction et au maintien du plan commercial, Réaliser les actions de prospection, Animer la relation commerciale avec les clients, prospects, les canaux de vente du groupe Orange et partenaires éditeurs, Définir et organiser les plans d’actions pour mener à bien les phases d’avant-vente, Assurer la présentation commerciale des offres tout au long du processus d’avant-vente, Détecter les opportunités commerciales chez les comptes clés afin de promouvoir nos offres digitales, Contrôler le respect des engagements d’Orange Business/ Digital Services France vis-à-vis des clients pour l’ensemble des activités de ses comptes clés, Effectuer les différents reporting d’activité commerciale.

title: Ingénieur(e) DevSecOps F/H
description: Description de l'entité Vous rejoignez l’équipe AOI, un groupe dynamique et engagé au sein de la Business Line Defense Center d’Orange. Cette équipe joue un rôle clé dans la sécurisation et l’innovation des solutions technologiques dédiées à la défense, en alliant expertise technique et esprit collaboratif pour relever les défis de demain. Description des missions DevSecOps : quand la sécurité rencontre l’automatisation, ça donne votre prochain défi ! Vos missions quotidiennes Vous prenez en charge la finalisation, le développement et la maintenance de la solution Qevlar AI, un outil d’investigation automatique des incidents de sécurité basé sur l’intelligence artificielle, déployé dans un environnement GCP et Kubernetes. Vous assurez la conception, le déploiement et la maintenance des pipelines CI/CD sécurisés pour garantir la qualité et la sécurité des livrables. Vous collaborez étroitement avec les équipes de développement et de sécurité pour intégrer les meilleures pratiques DevSecOps. Vous automatisez les processus de sécurité et de conformité tout au long du cycle de vie des applications. Vous surveillez et analysez les incidents de sécurité pour proposer des améliorations continues. Vous participez à la définition et à la mise en œuvre des politiques de sécurité dans un environnement cloud et on-premise. Vous pourrez être amené à travailler sur nos solutions d’orchestration, principalement XSOAR et N8N. Les compétences que vous aurez l’opportunité de développer grâce à ce poste Ce poste vous permettra de renforcer vos compétences techniques en DevSecOps, notamment dans l’intégration de solutions d’intelligence artificielle pour la sécurité, la gestion avancée des environnements cloud et l’automatisation des processus. Vous développerez également votre capacité à travailler dans un contexte exigeant et innovant, au sein d’une équipe collaborative et engagée.

title: Senior PR Executive
description: As Senior PR Executive, you will play a key role in delivering high-quality, impactful PR activity that strengthens apetito’s reputation across our priority markets. You will need a high degree of autonomy and judgement, and be trusted to lead the delivery of high-profile PR activity under the direction of the PR Manager. Sitting within the central Brand & Communications team, you will work closely with colleagues across the business and external partners to bring our stories to life, ensuring our voice is credible, consistent and values-led. You will also act as a role model within the PR team, helping to maintain high standards and support the development of others. Most importantly, you will help share how apetito and Wiltshire Farm Foods are making a real difference to customers, communities and colleagues every single day. At apetito | Wiltshire Farm Foods, we are on an ambitious journey. With bold growth targets ahead, how we tell our story and protect our reputation will play a critical role in helping us succeed. We are looking for someone with experience in PR and media relations, able to craft compelling stories as well as demonstrate the commercial impact of PR delivery. This role is based in our Trowbridge head office and you will need to be on site at least 3 days per week.

title: Ingénieur exploitation client F/H
description: Le poste de Security Operations Engineer est intégré au sein d’une équipe mutalisée. Il aura comme missions principales de : Garantir le bon fonctionnement des solutions opérationnelles des clients Prendre en charge les demandes clients et la résolution des incidents techniques complexes (niveau 2 / 3) Être un atout majeur sur des environnements de sécurité périmètriques, en accompagnant la montée en compétences de l’ensemble de l’équipe sur cette technologie En tant que tel, vous prendrez la responsabilité des tâches suivantes : Traiter les demandes clients : changements et/ou incidents complexes Identifier les problèmes et analyser leurs causes afin de proposer et mettre œuvre un plan d’actions correctif Gérer les escalades avec les clients et partenaires Maintenir la documentation client et rédiger les procédures d’exploitation

title: Ingénieur Développeur Confirmé
description: Conception Participation aux réunions fonctionnelles avec le Product Owner et autres parties prenantes Garantie de l'homogénéité des développements et de l'architecture en relation avec les autres équipes de la R&D Réalisation des spécifications techniques et participation aux analyses conceptuelles Chiffrage des charges de développement Développement Réalisation du développement, qualité de code Implémentation des tests unitaires Référent auprès que QA intégré à l’équipe de dev Participation aux différentes cérémonies d’un sprint Scrum : Daily Meeting, Sprint Review, etc. Production de la documentation technique du logiciel Rédaction et vérification des manuels d'utilisation et d'installation Reporting (dérives, problèmes techniques, ...) à l’équipe et autres parties prenantes Divers Formation et assistance aux services tels que : pôles expertise, intégration et support Assure la veille technologique Coordination entre l’équipe de développement et le Product Owner Reporting régulier de l'activité au Manager

title: Alternant consultant Transformation Organisationnelle F/H
description: Digital Services est l' ESN d'Orange Business qui combine les expertises Cloud, Digital, Data et IA. Plus de 4 000 experts répartis dans toute la France accompagnent des entreprises et institutions publiques dans leur transformation numérique. title: Team Leader - Meals
description: We’re the leading food producer for the health and social care sector, providing delicious, nutritious and sustainable meals for people at home or in care. We’re also the parent company of Wiltshire Farm Foods, the UK’s premier home-delivery service for ready-made meals. Through our wide range of well-presented, nutritious meals to suit every taste and dietary need, we aim to make a real difference to lives of elderly and vulnerable people in care. We’re proud to be a Living Wage employer and member of the Ethical Trading Initiative. We support British food and farming and operate a successful programme of waste reduction and resource efficiency across our whole business. In addition to your salary you will receive a comprehensive training program, 25 days annual leave holiday entitlement, the ability to buy additional annual leave, retail discounts, free car parking, subsidised staff restaurant, free turkey (or voucher) at Christmas and much more ! The department and team The main purpose of the Team Leader (Grade 6) is to lead, motivate, develop and encourage our team to deliver the daily production targets to an agreed standard. £17.10 per hour + Bonus + Benefits

description: We’re the leading food producer for the health and social care sector, providing delicious, nutritious and sustainable meals for people at home or in care. Each Runner is responsible for providing materials to their dedicated line, which will typically produce 20,000 meals per shift. Once a production run is complete, the Runner will then work with the team on the line changeover. The hours for this position are Monday to Friday 14:00-22:00 £14.78 per hour + Bonus + Benefits

title: Senior Product Developer
description: Can you bring exciting food to life and help create market‑leading meals and desserts? If you are inspired by all things food, thrive on solving problems, and get excited about turning ideas into reality, our Senior Product Developer role is for you. We are seeking an experienced Product Developer to join our passionate team of chefs, technologists and dietitians who deliver award‑winning innovation, acting as a development lead on one of our market leading business units across key projects and programmes. This is a role where creativity meets technical expertise and where, as the lead developer, your work truly makes a difference to our customers across the UK. You will play a pivotal part in leading and driving the Wiltshire Farm Foods product pipeline, improving current favourites, and pushing the boundaries of what great food can be. This is a hands‑on leadership role, where you will take ownership for delivering exceptional development projects and programmes from concept through to launch. You will confidently navigate the stage‑gate process, ensuring every product meets our standards, policies and customer expectations, while guiding and influencing stakeholders throughout development. You’ll identify emerging trends and opportunities, pitch exciting new ideas to the business, work closely with our Category Development team, and lead projects that enhance our customers’ experience and deliver competitive advantage. This role is based at our UK Headquarters in Trowbridge Wiltshire.

title: Transport & Operational Graduate
description: apetito are looking for a Transport & Operational Graduate to join the Distribution, Warehouse and Customer Service business unit based in Portbury, Bristol. We are seeking a dynamic and motivated individual to join our Warehouse and Transport team in an exciting rotational programme designed for growth and development into a management role. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone eager to learn and progress in a fast-paced, hands-on environment. As part of this role, you will rotate between various departments and key functions, gaining exposure to all aspects of warehouse operations, transport logistics, and customer service. Flexibility, adaptability, and a passion for leadership are essential as you grow with us and develop the skills necessary to step into a managerial role. We will create you a plan which will rotate you between customer service team, stock team, planning, driver +  Fleet Management and Warehousing. We will also be looking for you to focus on some specific projects as you complete these rotations. You will be reporting into the Warehouse manager you will work alongside all the senior management for the division. The operation is based at a National Distribution Centre, this incorporates a Frozen Storage and Order Fulfilment centre that operates 24 hours a day from Sunday Afternoon to Friday Night.  The core hours of operation for this role will be between 06:00 to 22:00 (Monday to Friday) in 8 hour shifts, with occasional requirement to work at night. This role will provide a graduate with: - Experience in a FMCG temperature controlled distribution operation - Exposure to the senior management team. - Opportunity to interact with and experience all parts of a business - Involvement with specific projects that they will be able to co-ordinate and play an active part in - The opportunity to develop general business skills and relationships

title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
description: This position is working 24 hours per week, any 3 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

Sr Electrical Project Engineer
description: Lead Innovative Electrical Design at HGA - Washington, DC or Boston, MA! Bring your passion and expertise in government, commercial, corporate, higher education, and/or performing arts projects to HGA , an award-winning, integrated architecture, engineering, and planning firm. As a Senior Electrical Project Engineer , you will play a pivotal role in leading and managing large-scale electrical projects and teams—locally and nationally. We’re looking for a highly organized, communicative, and effective team player with a passion for high-quality design and innovative problem-solving . If you thrive on leading teams, ensuring technical excellence, and delivering impactful designs, this is an exciting opportunity to grow your career. What You’ll Do: Lead electrical design on large-scale projects across various sectors, including corporate , higher education , and performing arts. Manage project teams locally and nationally to ensure high-quality, timely delivery of electrical systems and solutions. Develop and review electrical designs, including power distribution, lighting systems, and control systems, ensuring compliance with codes and client requirements. Provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior engineers and design professionals. Collaborate with architects, engineers, and other stakeholders to integrate electrical systems into innovative, functional designs. Communicate effectively with clients, consultants, and project teams to drive projects forward and ensure expectations are met. In this role you will: Assess the requirements of a project, break a project into tasks, and work with the design team to determine scope, budget, and staffing. Write scopes of work for electrical design based on preliminary review and meetings with the client. Prepare engineering studies and schematic design for new systems and analyze operation of existing systems. Present complex engineering analysis clearly and effectively to owner and design teams. Document existing conditions and perform complex and detailed troubleshooting. Resolve a variety of complex problems, i.e. conflicting design requirements, code issues, varying design options, and difficult condition requirements. Research non-routine code issues and obtain interpretations from code officials. Produce sketches and system schematics for complicated design tasks. Prepare engineering opinion of probable cost for electrical systems and review contractor cost estimates. Develop calculations using engineering formulas and skills for Electrical designs such as whole building electrical loads, lighting calculations, power distribution calculations, equipment sizing, editing specification sections, and creating punch lists. Review drawings and documents to assure compliance with company design and production standards. Assure that drawings and designs are in accordance with design standards. Develop and maintain a thorough understanding of construction processes and delivery methods. Assist in the education and development of design staff and act as a resource for questions. You will need to have: BS or MS degree in electrical engineering. Professional licensure is required. 12+ years of experience in leading design of government, commercial, corporate, higher education, and/or performing arts project. Documented experience with an MEP engineering firm or A/E firm providing electrical design. Expertise in National Fire Protection Association Codes (NFPA) and an understanding of applicable (city, state, federal) requirements and pertinent standards is essential. Active government security clearance, or eligibility to obtain and maintain the required clearance for project assignments. Working knowledge of Revit software. Familiarity with electrical analysis software (SKM, Easy Power) and knowledge of the current sustainable technologies applicable to the design of commercial electrical systems. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary range:  $121.000 - $147,800 depending on experience and expertise. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects, engineers, and interior designers — help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

title: Quality & Food Safety Auditor
description: The technical department can be described as the ‘Conscience of the Business’ with a core remit of keeping the business informed to ensure strong strategic and tactical decision, provide customer and supplier confidence in relation to food safety and quality standards and to safeguard the business by maintaining a strong due diligence defense. This is achieved by providing assurance that legal, customer, supplier and business standards of food quality and safety are maintained, ensuring procedural compliance, understanding and effectiveness and driving remedial actions to resolve the root cause of any issues and prevent recurrence. Additionally, the department has a responsibility to drive continuous improvement as an outcome of the core remit by setting standards and keeping abreast of developments that may impact the food industry. This role is working 36 hours per week, Friday - Sunday, 6am-6pm.

title: EMEA HE&R Region Lead
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: The EMEA Health Economics & Reimbursement (HE&R) Lead is a senior regional executive responsible for ensuring high‑quality access execution across the EMEA region. This includes leading a team of 6+ country HE&R Leads to deliver reimbursement, coverage, pricing, and reimbursement policy outcomes that enable sustainable access and drive business growth for Abbott’s medical device portfolio. This role serves as a critical partner to EMEA Divisional Vice Presidents, actively engaging to understand their business priorities, elevating clear access considerations, proactively identifying, evaluating and educating on shifting market dynamics, and delivering aligned and integrated action plans through regional HE&R business leaders and country teams. As a member of the HE&R Leadership Team, this role ensures that EMEA market requirements are incorporated into global product development, evidence strategies, and launch planning. When evidence or policy information is incomplete, this leader provides structured, risk‑adjusted recommendations to enable enterprise decision‑making; reflecting global HE&R leadership expectations for lifecycle integration. The role also partners closely with Government Affairs to monitor reimbursement‑relevant policy shifts, evaluate business impact, and shape Abbott’s external advocacy strategy, maintaining a targeted presence in regional professional societies and forums aligned with the EMEA access agenda. ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES Lead and supervise 6+ Country HE&R teams across EMEA, ensuring consistent, competitive, high‑quality execution of reimbursement and pricing strategies. Establish an operating rhythm for performance reviews, barrier escalation, best‑practice sharing, and launch readiness to ensure accountability and execution discipline. Ensure country-level plans are aligned to regional priorities and address local payer, HTA, and funding requirements. Proactively identify risks in access execution and drive cross-functional mitigation plans with clear owners and timelines Serve as a trusted access advisor to EMEA Divisional Vice Presidents, and partner to EMEA HE&R Regional Business Leaders, by translating business priorities into clear access considerations and in-market execution plans. Provide concise, actionable guidance on access trade-offs, portfolio prioritization, and resource needs. Support the development of access components of regional business plans with the regional HE&R business leads, ensuring alignment across Commercial, Medical, Clinical and Regulatory teams at the country level. Ensure country‑level pathway requirements are surfaced to Global/Regional Business Leads, and incorporated into local launch planning Ensure evidence requirements, payer expectations, and pathway prerequisites are systematically incorporated early into global and regional planning. When information is incomplete, lead structured risk assessments and propose risk-adjusted recommendations, including scenario assumptions and decision triggers. Partner with Government Affairs to track reimbursement, pricing, HTA, and funding policy dynamics across EMEA and translate developments into business impact assessments. Shape strategic responses and build business preparedness plans to navigate policy changes. Coordinate strategic functional representation in regional forums, societies, and coalitions aligned to advancing the EMEA access agenda Build, develop, and retain a high-performing team of country HE&R leaders and support their professional growth. Drive regional capability building in payer engagement, HTA navigation, economic value communication, and access-focused launch readiness. Provide clear performance expectations, coaching, succession planning, and accountability frameworks Oversee regional budget planning and resource allocation. Elevate access literacy across regional and country teams by providing structured guidance, tools, education and communication frameworks. Represent EMEA HE&R execution on the Global HE&R leadership team EDUCATION & COMPETENCIES Advanced degree in an associated field required (e.g., MPH, MBA, PhD, Health Economics, Public Policy, Health Services Research) 12+ years’ experience in Market Access, Reimbursement, Health Economics, Payer Strategy, or related roles in medical devices or healthcare; Previous experience in country level access leadership preferred Demonstrated success leading country access teams or driving access outcomes across multiple EMEA markets. Strong business partnership skills with the ability to influence senior leadership and shape strategy Proven ability to synthesize complex policy or evidence information into clear, actionable insights. Strong analytical, strategic thinking, and communication skills. Experience leading teams, developing talent, and fostering execution accountability. Ability to navigate complex matrix organizations and represent the function effectively in regional and global governance processes. SUCCESS METRICS (Internal) Execution of reimbursement and pricing strategy across priority EMEA markets. Achievement of competitive country-level access outcomes, including improved time-to-access metrics and reduced execution barriers. Regional business preparedness and ability to anticipate and respond to policy changes in collaboration with Government Affairs and key business partners. Quality of partnership with EMEA DVPs and cross-functional leaders, as reflected in alignment, execution discipline, and strategic influence. Integration of EMEA requirements into global evidence and product planning, including risk-adjusted recommendations when required. Team performance and capability growth, including retention and development of country HE&R leaders. Impact of external advocacy engagements aligned to the EMEA access agenda, reinforcing expectations from prior regional and global HE&R roles The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. title: Productie Planner
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: Over Abbott Abbott is een wereldwijde leider in de gezondheidszorg en zet zich in om mensen te helpen langer en beter te leven. Met ongeveer 130.000 medewerkers in meer dan 160 landen ontwikkelt Abbott een breed portfolio op het gebied van diagnostiek, medische hulpmiddelen, voeding en geneesmiddelen. In Nederland werken dagelijks circa 2.000 collega’s op 9 locaties aan duurzame oplossingen voor enkele van de belangrijkste uitdagingen binnen de gezondheidszorg van vandaag. Over Abbott Nutrition Abbott Nutrition levert wetenschappelijk onderbouwde voedingsproducten voor mensen van alle leeftijden. Onze producten ondersteunen de groei van kinderen en helpen te voorzien in de unieke voedingsbehoeften van mensen met chronische aandoeningen. Op onze productielocatie in Zwolle produceren wij verschillende vertrouwde voedingsmerken voor kinderen en volwassenen, waaronder PediaSure, Isomil en Ensure. Bij Abbott ontwikkelen we technologieën die levens veranderen en worden we gedreven door onze missie om voor iedereen, in elke levensfase, de juiste zorg te bieden. Wil jij deel uitmaken van deze missie en een bijdrage leveren aan de maatschappij? Ter versterking van onze productielocatie zijn wij op zoek naar een gemotiveerde en analytische Productieplanner die graag werkt op het snijvlak van productie, logistiek en service excellence. Dit betreft een kantoorfunctie in Zwolle. Wat ga je doen Als Productieplanner speel je een sleutelrol in het waarborgen van een betrouwbare supply chain en operationele continuïteit. Je werkt nauw samen met de lokale productieteams en het centrale planningsteam in Zwolle om alles soepel te laten verlopen. Je verantwoordelijkheden zijn onder andere: Monitoren en verbeteren van interne en externe servicelevels Dagelijkse afstemming met interne en externe stakeholders over prioriteiten binnen productie-, voorraad- en transportplanning Optimaliseren van analyses en KPI‑rapportages (bijvoorbeeld in Power BI) Fungeren als back‑up binnen het planningsteam om continuïteit te waarborgen Snel reageren op ad‑hoc verzoeken binnen de supply chain Wat breng je mee Een afgeronde hbo‑opleiding (Logistiek, Supply Chain Management, Bedrijfskunde of vergelijkbaar) Sterke analytische vaardigheden en een proactieve werkhouding Ervaring in productieplanning of supply chain is een sterke pré Goede communicatieve vaardigheden en het vermogen om cross‑functioneel samen te werken Flexibiliteit en veerkracht in een dynamische productieomgeving Uitstekende Excel‑vaardigheden; ervaring met Power BI is een plus Vloeiend in Nederlands en Engels (mondeling en schriftelijk) Beschikbaar voor een fulltime dienstverband (40 uur per week) Wat wij bieden Een uitdagende functie binnen een wereldwijde organisatie met een betekenisvolle missie Een professionele en samenwerkingsgerichte werkomgeving in Zwolle Ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, opleidingen en doorgroeimogelijkheden Een marktconform salaris en aantrekkelijke arbeidsvoorwaarden Een moderne werkomgeving waar initiatief en verbeterideeën worden gewaardeerd Solliciteren Ben je enthousiast over deze functie? Dien dan je sollicitatie in via de knop ‘Apply’ hierboven. Sollicitaties worden doorlopend beoordeeld. The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. Service Delivery Manager F/H
description: En tant que Service Delivery Manager, vous êtes l’interlocuteur privilégié du client dans le cadre d'un contrat de services. A ce titre, vous êtes le garant du respect des engagements, de la Qualité de Service globale et de la satisfaction client ainsi que de la rentabilité opérationnelle de ses comptes. Le périmètre fonctionnel des prestations attendues est décomposé en 4 lots : Lot 1 : BUILD Élaborer des solutions standard de cybersécurité et des services associés, sur la base des besoins exprimés par la Compagnie, à des coûts maîtrisés. Lot 2.1: DEPLOY Déployer des solutions et des services de cybersécurité sur nos sites industriels : installation/configuration, pilotage Lot 2.2: RUN Maintenir en conditions opérationnelles et de sécurité (MCO/MCS) les solutions et services de cybersécurité conformément aux SLA définis (Servie Level Agreement). Lot 3: Cyber Operations Assister les sites à mettre en oeuvre des mesures de remédiation lorsque des alertes et des incidents de cybersécurité sont signalés par le SIEM/SOC (Security Operation Centre) du client. Le Service Delivery Manager (SDM) assure l’interface entre le client et les différentes prestations des lots du dispositif, il est le point d’entrée qui peut solliciter tout interlocuteur interne pour répondre à la demande client. Vos missions : En relation direct avec les équipes d’exploitation, votre mission comprend les activités suivantes : Analyser les enjeux et criticités client au sein des équipes opérationnelles Piloter, dans certains cas, la mise en place du service managé (outils d’exploitation, supervision) Elaborer le reporting et assurer l’animation des comités Gérer et anticiper les situations de crise Garantir le respect des SLA (Service Level Agreement) auprès des clients Piloter les plans d’actions mis en place avec les équipes clients et les prestataires externes Suivre la satisfaction client, proposer un plan d’amélioration continu et optimiser les services contractualisés Assurer un rôle de conseil et participer au développement de la relation commerciale en identifiant les opportunités business et en les adressant aux équipes commerciales. Rendre compte de son activité et alerter si nécessaire La langue de communication orale pour la gouvernance du contrat sera le français. L’anglais sera utilisé dans les correspondances avec les sites, hormis pour les sites francophones. Tous les documents et livrables écrits seront rédigés en anglais.

title: Technical Compliance  Manager
description: At apetito, we are committed to delivering the highest standards of food safety, quality and compliance. We’re looking for an experienced Technical Compliance Manager to help us maintain and continually improve these standards. As Technical Compliance Manager, you will lead the validation, verification and audit of our UK food safety and quality systems. You’ll work closely with technical and operational teams to ensure full compliance with legal requirements and maintain our AA grade BRCGS Food Safety certification. This is a fantastic time to join us. As we grow towards £300m in turnover, we’re investing in a new, state‑of‑the‑art frozen food manufacturing facility alongside our existing site in Trowbridge. Your responsibilities will cover both sites, offering significant opportunity for development as our operations expand.

title: Content Writer - Retailer (3rd party)
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: GIỚI THIỆU VỀ ABBOTT Abbott là công ty chăm sóc sức khỏe toàn cầu, tiên phong trong nghiên cứu khoa học và tạo ra những đột phá trong công nghệ chăm sóc sức khỏe để giúp mọi người sống khỏe mạnh hơn. Chúng tôi luôn hướng tới tương lai, dự đoán và đáp ứng những thay đổi trong khoa học và công nghệ y khoa. Những công nghệ mang tính thay đổi cuộc sống của Abbott trải dài nhiều lĩnh vực từ dinh dưỡng, chẩn đoán đến thiết bị y tế và dược phẩm. LÀM VIỆC TẠI ABBOTT Tại Abbott, bạn có thể làm công việc tạo ra giá trị và ý nghĩa cho cộng đồng. Bạn được trao cơ hội để học hỏi và phát triển, chăm sóc bản thân và gia đình, đồng thời thể hiện và phát huy tối đa tiềm năng của bản thân trong cả sự nghiệp và cuộc sống. Bạn sẽ có cơ hội: Phát triển sự nghiệp tại một công ty toàn cầu, nơi bạn có thể hiện thực hóa ước mơ nghề nghiệp của mình. Làm việc tại một môi trường được công nhận là “Nơi làm việc tốt nhất” tại nhiều quốc gia và được tạp chí Fortune (Mỹ) vinh danh là một trong những công ty được ngưỡng mộ nhất thế giới. Trở thành một phần của doanh nghiệp hàng đầu, được công nhận là nơi làm việc tốt nhất với môi trường tuyệt vời cho sự đa dạng và hòa nhập. Địa điểm: Flemington Tower, 182 Le Dai Hanh, Q11, HCM Hình thức hợp đồng: Dịch vụ bên thứ ba (Third-party services) Thời gian làm việc: Thứ Hai – Thứ Bảy (thời gian linh hoạt theo tính chất campaign) CONTENT WRITER Nhánh Đại lý (Retailer) GIỚI THIỆU CHUNG Content Writer - chịu trách nhiệm xây dựng và phát triển nội dung số hỗ trợ hệ thống đại lý (Retailer) của Abbott,  chuyển đổi từ bán hàng truyền thống lên online. Yêu cầu tư duy sáng tạo, am hiểu nền tảng TikTok & Facebook, có khả năng sáng tạo kịch bản video, xây dựng content hub và định hướng phát triển kênh cho đại lý. Giúp đại lý nâng cao năng lực bán hàng online và mở rộng tệp khách hàng qua các nền tảng số thông quan content. MỤC TIÊU VỊ TRÍ Hỗ trợ hệ thống đại lý chuyển đổi thành công từ mô hình bán hàng truyền thống sang online. Xây dựng hệ thống nội dung chuẩn, dễ nhân bản cho đại lý áp dụng trên TikTok & Facebook. Nâng cao năng lực phát triển kênh và bán hàng online của đại lý. Tăng lượng tiếp cận organic và mở rộng tệp khách hàng mới cho hệ thống đại lý. NỘI DUNG CÔNG VIỆC CHÍNH A. Xây dựng kỹ năng nền cho đại lý khi chuyển đổi từ truyền thống lên online Xây dựng content hub (kho nội dung mẫu) để đại lý dễ dàng tham khảo và áp dụng cho kênh bán hàng online. Xây dựng kỹ năng livestream cho đại lý: hướng dẫn kịch bản, cách tương tác, kỹ thuật chốt đơn trong livestream. Sáng tạo kịch bản video xây kênh và bán hàng phù hợp với từng nhóm đại lý và sản phẩm. Theo dõi, đánh giá và feedback về chất lượng video của đại lý; nhân bản những video tốt để lan tỏa trong hệ thống. B. Nâng cao năng lực phát triển kênh bán hàng của đại lý Định hướng chiến lược phát triển kênh TikTok & Facebook cho từng đại lý. Lập kế hoạch nội dung (content calendar) dài hạn, đảm bảo tính nhất quán và hiệu quả. Phát triển nội dung tăng lượng tiếp cận organic, mở rộng tệp khách hàng tiềm năng. Cập nhật xu hướng, algorithm changes và best practices trên các nền tảng. C. Phối hợp và đào tạo Phối hợp với team marketing và đội ngũ đại lý để triển khai nội dung đúng hướng và đúng tiến độ. Hỗ trợ đào tạo đại lý về kỹ năng sáng tạo nội dung, quay video và livestream. Đo lường hiệu quả nội dung, rút ra insight để cải thiện liên tục. YÊU CẦU Kinh nghiệm & Trình độ 2-3 năm kinh nghiệm viết content và sản xuất nội dung số, ưu tiên có kinh nghiệm với short video (TikTok, Facebook Reels). Có kinh nghiệm viết kịch bản video bán hàng, livestream hoặc xây kênh là lợi thế lớn. Kinh nghiệm làm việc trong ngành FMCG, dinh dưỡng, sức khỏe hoặc Mẹ & Bé là điểm cộng. Kỹ năng chuyên môn Tư duy content marketing mạnh, hiểu customer journey và conversion funnel. Khả năng viết kịch bản video hấp dẫn, tự nhiên, phù hợp với phong cách nền tảng (native content). Thành thạo các công cụ: Capcut, Canva,… Nắm bắt nhanh xu hướng nền tảng, algorithm và hành vi người dùng trên TikTok & Facebook. Tố chất cá nhân Chủ động, trách nhiệm cao, có tinh thần hợp tác. Giao tiếp tốt, có khả năng làm việc với nhiều nhóm đại lý khác nhau. Bắt trend nhanh, thích ứng linh hoạt với thay đổi của thị trường. Đam mê digital marketing và content creation. description: Project Architect – Community + Live, Workplace & Government (CWG) markets - Sacramento, CA HGA, a leading architectural, engineering, and planning firm, is seeking a talented and driven Project Architect to join our growing CWG practice in Sacramento, CA. We’re looking for someone with strong communication, presentation, time management, and interpersonal skills who thrives in a collaborative environment and is passionate about advancing great design. In this role, you’ll work closely with Senior Designers, Project Managers, and other Project Architects to develop innovative and technically sound design solutions. You’ll lead the creation of detailed documentation while maintaining our firm’s high standards for design quality and client service. What You’ll Do: Lead the development of construction documents and ensure design intent is accurately carried through. Translate complex project goals into creative and functional design solutions. Collaborate across disciplines to coordinate architectural and engineering systems. Participate in client meetings and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders. Ensure project milestones and deadlines are met with attention to detail and quality. Why HGA? Collaborative Culture: Join a firm celebrated for integrated, cross‑disciplinary teamwork and a shared commitment to design excellence. Professional Growth: Build your career with mentorship, continuing education, and clear pathways to leadership. Meaningful Work: Shape the environments where people live, work, and play through projects that make a lasting impact. In this role you will: Design and u nderstand and contribute to the project's design philosophy. Provide major input in developing design and building performance strategies and convey these effectively to team members. Lead project teams to develop complete document sets based on the project phase. Provide technical design input and solutions to project challenges, supporting the overall design concept. Oversee technical design efforts, detailed design development, project coordination, and quality control during all project phases, collaborating closely with the Senior Designer. Execute applicable agency review analysis (accessibility, zoning, life-safety, etc.). Actively collaborate with and coordinate the work of consultants and knowledge resources to ensure project deliverables are coordinated, integrated, and support the design concept and performance goals. Set an example for team effort through the spirit of cooperation while directing, organizing, and mentoring junior staff. C ontribute to the preparation of design documents and lead the detailed development of construction documents during all project phases. Ensure complete, accurate, and coordinated project documentation. Oversee and perform construction administration duties, including reviewing shop drawings, material samples, RFI’s, RFP’s, and change orders for conformance with design. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in Architecture California Architectural Licensure required LEED accreditation preferred 7 - 10 years of experience, including work on small to mid-size projects Deep understanding of programming, codes, and technical detailing Strong leadership and collaboration skills in team environments Proficiency in Revit required; experience with AutoCAD, SketchUp, Enscape, and other graphic tools desired. Expertise with project management software and Microsoft Office Suite products needed Excellent communication, presentation, and time management skills Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $98,300 - $115,000 depending on qualifications and experience . Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects, engineers, and interior designers — help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume and portfolio for consideration.

AMOA SIRH - Paie, GA et DSN F/H
description: Le SIRH d’OBS SA est constitué de plusieurs solutions SI permettant de supporter les processus RH de l’entreprise. Ces solutions adressent l’ensemble des processus RH, du recrutement à la gestion opérationnelle des salariés (la gestion administrative, la gestion des temps, la paie et l’ensemble du Talent Management). Ces processus doivent répondre aux objectifs d’excellence d’OBS SA aussi bien au niveau de l’expérience salarié qu’à celui de la performance opérationnelle des acteurs RH. Le rôle de l’équipe SIRH est de mettre à la disposition du réseau RH et/ou des salariés et managers de l’entreprise, des outils modernes et performants leur permettant de mener à bien leur mission. Elle est le garant du bon fonctionnement des systèmes d’informations RH (le SIRH) mais aussi est force de proposition pour l’adaptation des outils, en adéquation avec les évolutions des besoins des utilisateurs. La gestion de la paie / GA et DSN est réalisée sur l’outil HR Access V9 avec un portail collaborateur permettant la mise à jour de certaines données directement par les collaborateurs. L’application permet une gestion fluide et intégrée de la paie et du déclaratif. Nous sommes à la recherche de notre futur(e) AMOA SIRH – Paie / GA / DSN. Vous interviendrez sur les applications liées à la paie , à la gestion administrative et à la DSN principalement sur la partie RUN. Votre rôle sera, en appui au responsable SIRH Paie d’assurer l’expertise, la conformité réglementaire du RUN et de participer éventuellement à des projets annexes impactant l’univers SIRH Paie. Vous serez accompagné sur votre périmètre par le responsable SIRH en binôme et vous serez en soutien auprès des équipes métier (équipes de paie, administration du personnel) dont l’objectif primordial est la satisfaction de nos clients (salariés) via l’excellence de la gestion de la paie et de leur expérience utilisateur. Vous aurez comme principales missions : Gestion RUN et optimisation des processus de paie : Vous assurerez le suivi du run de paie, la gestion des anomalies de paie ainsi que le suivi des évolutions. Vous veillerez donc à garantir la fluidité des processus et à améliorer leur efficacité en définissant et rédigeant les spécifications générales et détaillées des évolutions légales / réglementaires et/ou spécifiques à mettre en place. Veille et conformité règlementaire : Vous effectuerez une veille légale et conventionnelle constante afin de vous assurer que les processus de paie sont conformes aux exigences réglementaires. Vous accompagnerez les gestionnaires de paie dans les adaptations nécessaires et serez garant(e) de la mise en conformité des processus de paie en qualifiant et validant les recettes fonctionnelles réalisées par l’éditeur. Accompagnement des utilisateurs : Vous assurerez une communication régulière auprès des utilisateurs concernant les nouveautés, les incidents, et les mises à jour applicatives. Vous vulgariserez les évolutions légales auprès d’un public averti en fournissant des procédures, des guides et vous organiserez des formations pour accompagner leur montée en compétences. Ce processus d’accompagnement sera aussi réalisé par une assistance multiple (ticketing) de niveau 2 ou 3 de nos Keys Users.

title: Health & Safety Administrator
description: Are you an experienced administrator ready for a new challenge? We are seeking a skilled administrator to support our team of seven safety, health, fire and environmental specialists, helping protect and enhance standards across the entire apetito organisation. With nearly 2,000 employees, plus contractors, operating across around 50 sites — and with continued growth on the horizon — maintaining robust safety, fire prevention and environmental performance is central to how we operate. Ensuring our people stay safe and that we remain a responsible neighbour in every community is an essential part of our mission. In this role, you’ll be at the centre of the team’s work, providing the practical, day‑to‑day support that helps our specialists operate more efficiently and effectively. The variety is broad, the pace is lively and your contribution will be critical to everything we deliver. title: B2B2C Delivery Executive
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: ABOUT ABBOTT Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as a best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists. MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Delivery Execution & Monitoring • Monitor daily B2B2C order fulfillment from CLPs to Shipping Service Provider of all platforms. • Track delivery performance against SLA, lead time, Shop Health of fulfillment, and service KPIs across O2O operations. • Ensure timely handover of orders from CLPs to shipping providers. • Follow up the return package handed over to CLPs on time. • Follow up on daily fulfillment execution, identifying risks that may impact delivery performance 2. Logistics Shipping Provider Coordination • Coordinate with delivery service providers (e.g. Viettel Post, J&T Express,…) to ensure on time pickups and deliveries. • Align pickup schedules and volume forecasts with logistics partners to support smooth operations. • Follow up with shipping providers to ensure platform SLA compliance. • Escalate and resolve delivery delays, exceptions, or failed deliveries. 3. Internal & External Coordination Work closely with O2O, 3A Logistics, CLPs, Sales & Trade, Platforms to: • Ensure order readiness and inventory availability • Confirm pickup timing • Address operational gaps impacting fulfillment and delivery • Provide daily operational support to CLPs to ensure orders are processed and delivered on time. • Coordinate with internal functions to ensure store onboarding is completed on time with full operational awareness. • Train CLPs on standard packaging and fulfillment requirements for each platform to ensure consistent execution and compliance. 4. Performance Tracking, Inventory & Reporting • Consolidate and prepare daily / weekly supply chain and delivery reports (SLA, backlog, exceptions). • Monitor and follow up on daily inventory status (products & gifts) at store level in line with demand planning. • Track order management and fulfillment performance, working with cross functional teams to address gaps. • Support performance reviews with internal stakeholders and logistics partners. • Coordinate the development and rollout of standard O2O fulfillment reports. 5. Issue Resolution & Continuous Improvement • Manage delivery related incidents end to end, including: Fast delivery rate, late delivery rate, cross-region rate, • Identify recurring fulfillment and delivery issues and propose corrective and preventive actions. • Support the rollout of new supply chain systems and tools, acting as a key user / SME for order management. • Support the Manager in reviewing, sharpening, and improving fulfillment SOPs to enhance operational efficiency and standardization. MINIMUM BACKGROUND/ EXPERIENCE REQUIRED: • Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business, or related fields • Project Management & Supply Chain Management • 1–3 years of experience in delivery operations, logistics coordination, or e commerce fulfillment • Experience with B2B2C / O2O / last mile delivery is an advantage • Strong coordination and follow up skills • Good data tracking and reporting capability • Ability to work with multiple stakeholders under time pressure • Problem solving and execution oriented mindset • Teamwork and cooperation • English skill in intermediate • Good skill on Power BI, Power Point & Excel. description: Vos missions : En mission interne, vous interviendrez au sein du SOC d'un de nos clients. L’objectif du service Security Operation Center est de lutter contre la cybercriminalité en mettant en œuvre les solutions techniques ou fonctionnelles nécessaires à la détection des tentatives d’intrusions sur le SI, le réseau, les plates-formes de services et les infrastructures. Le service a donc une vision transverse sur les menaces adressant les collaborateurs mais aussi les outils de production de ce client. La mission est orientée en différentes activités : Gestion des événements de sécurité (RUN) : en tant qu'analyste SOC, vous assurez la sécurité défensive de ce client au sein d’une équipe d’ analystes. Vous avez un rôle d'expertise sur le traitement des incidents de sécurité. Vous aurez en charge : La surveillance et le traitement des évènements et des alertes de sécurité ; L'analyse des données et l'investigation avancée des incidents ; La coordination avec l'ensemble des acteurs du client pour la résolution des incidents et l'accompagnement à la remédiation ; La participation au reporting des incidents de sécurité, de l'activité opérationnelle et la fourniture d'indicateurs ; Amélioration continue : gestion des changements et optimisations : En tant qu'analyste SOC, vous êtes acteur de l’amélioration continue. Vous aurez en charge en lien avec notre Security Manager : L'amélioration continue des systèmes de corrélation de la sécurité ; Les optimisations en matière de détection des menaces ; La veille technologique des menaces, des attaques et des vulnérabilités. Accompagnement de la croissance du périmètre et des évolutions technologiques du SOC : le périmètre du SOC est voué à croître suivant le planning d'intégration de notre client. En tant qu’analyste SOC, vous serez acteur de cette croissance en réalisant : L'intégration de nouveaux périmètres au sein de la détection du SOC La proposition et mise en œuvre de nouvelles règles de détection L'intégration de nouveaux outils au sein de l'activité du SOC Les technologies de détection utilisées par notre client évoluent : en tant qu’analyste SOC, vous êtes acteur de la migration technologique et de la continuité de service en vous formant à des nouveaux outils de manière régulière. Pour assurer la continuité de service de notre client, des astreintes sont à réaliser. Afin de vous accompagner dans cette mission, vous serez en lien direct avec les équipes du CyberSOC d'Orange Cyberdefense qui vous permettront de progresser sur les différents outils et méthodes de traitement des incidents de sécurité. Le poste nécessite de satisfaire aux obligations relatives à la Protection du Secret de la Défense Nationale. Ce poste peut être soumis, selon le besoin, à une enquête administrative.

description: Physician, Pediatric Endocrinology MemorialCare Medical Group Practice Location Cherese Mari Laulhere Children’s Village Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital Long Beach / MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center Campus Long Beach, California Position Summary MemorialCare Medical Group is seeking a BC/BE Pediatric Endocrinologist to provide high-quality, family-centered care in a community-based pediatric specialty practice. This position is based at the Cherese Mari Laulhere Children’s Village at Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital Long Beach, located on the campus of MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center. The Pediatric Endocrinologist provides comprehensive specialty medical care to pediatric patients with endocrine and metabolic disorders in both ambulatory and inpatient settings. The role includes evaluation, diagnosis, treatment, and ongoing management of conditions within the scope of pediatric endocrinology, consistent with MemorialCare Medical Group clinical standards, policies, and applicable regulations. Essential Functions and Responsibilities Clinical Care Evaluate, diagnose, and manage pediatric patients with endocrine and metabolic conditions including diabetes mellitus, thyroid disease, growth and pubertal disorders, adrenal and pituitary diseases, disorders of sexual development, and calcium/bone metabolism disorders. Provide outpatient ambulatory care including initial consultations, longitudinal management, follow-up visits, and care coordination. Provide inpatient consultation services for hospitalized pediatric patients and participate in the assigned call schedule. Develop and implement individualized treatment plans in accordance with current standards of pediatric endocrine care. Order, interpret, and act upon laboratory, imaging, and diagnostic studies relevant to pediatric endocrinology. Prescribe medications, therapies, and medical devices (e.g., insulin pumps, continuous glucose monitoring) as clinically indicated. Document encounters accurately and timely in the electronic health record (EHR) in accordance with policy and regulatory requirements. Collaboration and Care Coordination Collaborate with neonatologists, pediatric intensivists, pediatric emergency medicine physicians, and other other physician colleagues, nurses, diabetes educators, dietitians, social workers, and other allied health professionals to ensure coordinated, family-centered care. Participate in interdisciplinary case discussions, clinical pathways, and care planning as appropriate. Communicate effectively with patients, families, and caregivers regarding diagnoses, treatment plans, and disease management. Quality, Compliance, and Professional Practice Adhere to MemorialCare Medical Group policies, clinical protocols, and quality standards. Comply with federal, state, and local regulations, including documentation, privacy, coding, and billing requirements. Participate in quality improvement, patient safety, and performance improvement initiatives as assigned. Maintain current knowledge of developments in pediatric endocrinology and engage in continuing medical education (CME). Participate in medical staff meetings, committees, and other administrative duties as required. Teaching and Program Support (as applicable) Participate in teaching and mentoring of medical students, residents, or fellows, if applicable. Support development of pediatric endocrine programs, pathways, and clinical initiatives. Qualifications Required MD or DO from an accredited medical school. Completion of an accredited Pediatric Residency Program. Completion of an accredited Pediatric Endocrinology Fellowship Program. Board Certification/Board Eligibility in Pediatric Endocrinology by the American Board of Pediatrics (ABP). Active, unrestricted California medical license (or eligibility to obtain prior to start). Current DEA registration. Eligibility for and maintenance of hospital medical staff privileges at MemorialCare-affiliated facilities. · Preferred Clinical experience in both inpatient and outpatient pediatric endocrinology settings. Experience with diabetes technologies, including insulin pumps and continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Experience in multidisciplinary pediatric care and population health initiatives. Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Comprehensive knowledge of pediatric endocrine diseases and standards of care. Ability to provide age-appropriate, family-centered care. Strong clinical judgment and diagnostic decision-making skills. Effective written and verbal communication skills. Ability to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary healthcare team. Proficiency with EHR systems and clinical documentation. Commitment to professionalism, ethical practice, and continuous improvement. Work Environment Work is performed in clinical settings including outpatient clinics and hospital inpatient units. May involve exposure to communicable diseases, bodily fluids, chemicals, and other hazards typical of a healthcare environment. Requires adherence to infection prevention and safety protocols. Physical Requirements Ability to sit, stand, and walk for extended periods within clinical and hospital environments. Ability to perform physical examinations and clinical procedures typical of pediatric endocrine practice. Visual, auditory, and motor skills sufficient to provide safe and effective patient care. Ability to work extended hours, evenings, weekends, and call shifts as necessary. May include travel to outpatient specialty clinics such as Torrance and Fountain Valley. Schedule and Call This position includes scheduled ambulatory clinic sessions and inpatient consultation coverage. Participation in the division call schedule is required; specific rotation and frequency are determined by departmental needs. Supervisory Responsibility May provide clinical direction to residents, students, nursing staff, diabetic educators, and allied health staff as applicable. May serve as a supervising physician for the Group’s Advanced Health Practitioner(s) in Pediatric Endocrinology, as applicable. Compensation and Benefits Competitive compensation package with a base salary and performance-based compensation. Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with matching, paid time off, and CME allowance.
