description: JOB DESCRIPTION: About Abbott Abbott is a global healthcare leader, creating breakthrough science to improve people’s health. We’re always looking towards the future, anticipating changes in medical science and technology. Working at Abbott At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and family, be your true self and live a full life. You will have access to: Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of. A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries around the world and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune. A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as a best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists. Our nutrition business develops science-based nutrition products for people of all ages, from helping babies and children grow, to keeping adult bodies strong and active. Millions of people around the world count on our leading brands – including Similac®, PediaSure®, Pedialyte®, Ensure®, Glucerna® and ZonePerfect® – to help get the nutrients they need to live their healthiest life. Job summary: Internal job title: Seller Growth Executive Job Function / Department: Online to Offline (O2O) Report to: Seller Growth Manager Working location: Flemington Tower - 182 Le Dai Hanh, District 11, HCMC Contract: Third-party contract Explain acronyms: O2O = Online-to-Offline (mô hình kết hợp bán hàng online và offline). SKU = Stock Keeping Unit (mã định danh sản phẩm) CLP = Customer Loyalty Program (Khách hàng trung thành) P&L = Profit and Loss (báo cáo hoặc quản lý lợi nhuận và chi phí). New model instruction: O2O (Online-to-Offline) connects online platforms with offline experiences. Customers discover products online (via ads, livestreams) and complete purchases through offline channels or integrated delivery, creating a seamless shopping journey. MAIN PURPOSE OF ROLE Summarize the main purpose of the role: • Work closely with Enablers(Agency) and CLPs to achieve goals/targets • Support Seller Growth Manager on daily/weekly business updates • Ensure all execution planned tasks delivered MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES Shopee Virtual Store: • Conduct in-depth sales data analysis to develop an optimized assortment and commercial plan • Collaborate closely with key stakeholders to ensure seamless execution of the plan, including product listing and pricing, stock fulfilment, shop content production (livestream/videos), Ad optimization, Order management, customer services • Provide weekly performance reports and daily run-rate updates to track progress and deliver a comprehensive business overview Seller Satellite program : A program managed by an agency to enhance online selling capabilities for CLPs • Manage the CLP community and share weekly updates on program developments • Partner with platform Account Managers to secure benefits and support, including violation appeals for CLPs under the program • Coordinate with enablers to ensure complete support for CLPs, covering Ad optimization, Content production, Commercial planning The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. JOB FAMILY: Sales Force DIVISION: LOCATION: Vietnam > Ho Chi Minh City > Flemington Tower ADDITIONAL LOCATIONS: WORK SHIFT: Standard TRAVEL: Not specified MEDICAL SURVEILLANCE: Not Applicable SIGNIFICANT WORK ACTIVITIES: Continuous sitting for prolonged periods (more than 2 consecutive hours in an 8 hour day)

title: Industrial Engineer
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: About Abbott Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritionals and branded generic medicines. Our 114,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. Abbott in Ireland Abbott has been operating in Ireland since 1946 and serves the Irish market with a diverse range of healthcare products including diagnostics, diabetes care, medical devices and nutritional products. In Ireland, Abbott currently employs about 6,000 people across ten sites located in Dublin, Donegal, Clonmel, Cootehill, Galway, Kilkenny, Longford and Sligo. Please note any offer of employment for this position is conditional upon the successful completion of a pre-employment medical examination, including drug and alcohol screening, conducted by a medical practitioner designated by the Company. Abbott Diabetes Care Kilkenny Abbott Diabetes Care has opened a new site, in Kilkenny, that is at the forefront of Diabetes care with the latest technology, to manufacture FreeStyle Libre 3 blood glucose testing in diabetes management. Among Abbott Diabetes Care leading brands are the FreeStyle Precision, FreeStyle Lite and FreeStyle Libre systems . MAIN PURPOSE OF ROLE In this role, you will be responsible for delivering immediate operational impact by optimizing the utilization of personnel, processes, and equipment within Operations. You will apply Lean Six Sigma tools to drive near‑term improvements, including process flow development, value stream mapping, time studies, line balancing, and the reduction of material and labour waste. You will update and develop standard work, analyse headcount versus capacity models, and embed Lean and MOS principles into existing facility infrastructure. Through the application of lean fundamentals, you will improve quality, cost, safety, and delivery performance across operations. In parallel, you will build and own a medium to long‑term operational excellence strategy, translating business and growth objectives into a clearly defined roadmap for future manufacturing layouts, capability development, and facility evolution. This strategy will ensure sustainable performance improvements, scalability, and alignment with organisational priorities, while enabling continuous improvement to be embedded across Operations over time. An Industrial Engineer’s role isn’t just to improve metrics—it’s to ensure the system delivers: Faster lead time Higher quality Lower cost Better experience MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES Responsible for identifying and implementing improvements to strategic site operations and processes to support the delivery of site goals. Lead industrial engineering activities relating to clean room and equipment expansion programs and achievement of planned capacity. Apply engineering methods to deliver models and presentations relating to facility, capacity and expansion planning and recommendations to management. Measure and report on efficiency data. Maintain oversight of manufacturing data routinely collected to ensure necessary data is available to inform strategic decision making. Use influencing skills to ensure outcomes of analysis are implemented to good effect. Recommend and develop manufacturing methods and tools that support and promote improvements in the utilisation of people, equipment, processes materials and utilities across all business processes. Establish, set and maintain process standards and the capacity book, measuring performance to plan. Deliver solutions to address unfavourable variances. Through knowledge and Lean Sigma tools identify and deliver improvements to reduce waste and improve process and system performance. Analyse capacity requirements to achieve output, propose labour and equipment resources to achieve business plans. Develop and lead continuous improvement projects, working cross functionally to implement changes that deliver savings across the business. Work closely with Systems and Process Engineers to develop and improve dashboard visualisation to support manufacturing teams. Note: This job description describes the principal and main elements of the job. It is a guide to the nature and main duties of the job as they currently exist but it is not intended as a wholly or comprehensive or permanent representation. Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s degree in engineering / science. +3 Years Relevant Experience, In-depth knowledge and application of industrial engineering techniques, measurement of OEE, and knowledge of Lean Sigma tools. Black Belt Qualification. Experience with overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) data management, statistical process control (SPC), financial budgets/standards. High degree of technical expertise and familiarity with manufacturing processes. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Proficiency in project management and mathematical modelling. Knowledge of Lean principles, Six Sigma, and other improvement methodologies. Ability to work within a team and as an individual contributor in a fast-paced, changing environment. Strong verbal and written communications with ability to effectively communicate at multiple levels in the organization. Key Skillset Lean and Six Sigma Data Analytics and Statistics Project Management Critical Thinking and RCA Workflow and System Design Strong communication and facilitation End to End Thinking What we Offer Attractive compensation package that includes competitive pay, as well as benefits such as Family health insurance, Excellent pension scheme Life assurance Career Development Fantastic new facility Growing business plus access to many more benefits. Connect with us at www.abbott.com or https://www.ie.abbott/, on LinkedIn at www.linkedin.com/company/abbott-/, on Facebook at www.facebook.com/Abbott and on Twitter @AbbottNews. Abbott is an equal opportunities employer. The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. JOB FAMILY: Manufacturing DIVISION: LOCATION: Ireland > Kilkenny : Kilkenny Business and Technology Park ADDITIONAL LOCATIONS: WORK SHIFT: Standard TRAVEL: Not specified MEDICAL SURVEILLANCE: Not Applicable SIGNIFICANT WORK ACTIVITIES: Not Applicable

title: Practicante de EHS
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: Acerca de Abbott Abbott es líder mundial en cuidado de la salud, que crea ciencia innovadora para mejorar la salud de las personas.  Siempre estamos mirando hacia el futuro, anticipando cambios en la ciencia y la tecnología médica. Trabajando en Abbott En Abbott, puedes hacer un trabajo que importa, crecer y aprender, cuidar de ti mismo y de tu familia, ser verdaderamente quien es y vivir una vida plena. Tendrás acceso a: Desarrollo profesional con una empresa internacional donde podrás hacer crecer la carrera que sueñas. Una compañía reconocida como mejor lugar para trabajar en docenas de países alrededor del mundo y nombrada una de las empresas más admiradas del mundo por Fortune. Una compañía que es reconocida como una de las mejores compañías grandes para trabajar, así como un mejor lugar para trabajar para la diversidad, las madres trabajadoras, mujeres ejecutivas y científicas. Que harás El practicante de EHS es el responsable de dar soporte a la implementación del Sistema de Gestión de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo en la filial asignada de Perú, alineando los estándares corporativos en materia con la legislación vigente del país. Es responsable de facilitar, gestionar y soportar la gestión de los peligros y la evaluación de los riesgos en las actividades laborales con el fin de reducir los incidentes, accidentes y enfermedades de índole laboral. Algunas de las responsabilidades incluyen: Dar Soporte en la implementación, planificación, ejecución, mantenimiento y mejora del Sistema de Gestión de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo exigido por normas locales y estándares corporativos. Soporte en la creación y actualización de los documentos exigidos por ley en materia de seguridad y salud en el trabajo, tales como, el Plan Anual de Trabajo, Plan Estratégico, Reglamento Interno de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo, la Política, entre otros. description: JOB DESCRIPTION: MBO-stage bij Abbott in Weesp Ben jij op zoek naar een uitdagende stageplek waar je kunt werken aan je vaardigheden en kennis binnen de farmaceutische industrie? Abbott in Weesp biedt een interessante stageplek voor een MBO-student die graag wil leren en zichzelf verder wil ontwikkelen. Wat ga je doen? Als stagiair bij Abbott ga je aan de slag op een van onze afdelingen, afhankelijk van jouw opleiding en interesses. Hier krijg je de kans om praktijkervaring op te doen en te leren van ervaren professionals binnen het vakgebied. Je zult betrokken worden bij verschillende projecten en werkzaamheden, waarbij je de mogelijkheid hebt om jouw kennis toe te passen en nieuwe vaardigheden te ontwikkelen. Wat bieden wij? Abbott biedt een dynamische en uitdagende werkomgeving waar je als stagiair volop kunt groeien en leren. Naast de kans om praktijkervaring op te doen, krijg je bij ons de mogelijkheid om jouw theoretische kennis toe te passen en verder te ontwikkelen. Bovendien kom je terecht in een hecht team van professionals die jou graag willen ondersteunen en begeleiden tijdens jouw stage. Bonus: Wij bieden een salaris van 500 euro per maand! Wie zoeken wij? Wij zijn op zoek naar een enthousiaste 3e/ 4e jaars MBO-student die zichzelf verder wil ontwikkelen binnen de farmaceutische industrie. Je volgt een relevante opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in de richting van laboratoriumtechniek of procestechniek, en hebt een grote interesse in dit vakgebied. Je bent leergierig, proactief en kunt goed samenwerken in een team. Heb je interesse? Ben je geïnteresseerd in deze stageplek bij Abbott in Weesp? Solliciteer direct en wie weet verwelkomen we jou binnenkort als stagiair binnen ons team! The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. Sr Editorial Writer
description: Are you a sharp, adaptable writer who can transform complex ideas into clear, compelling narratives? Do you believe great writing can shape perception, strengthen a brand, and build meaningful connections with audiences? HGA is looking for an Editorial & Brand Writer to help bring our voice to life across channels, audiences, and markets. As part of HGA’s Digital Marketing & Content Strategy team, this role plays a central part in defining how we tell our story as a national interdisciplinary design firm. You’ll craft, refine, and elevate content that advances brand visibility, market positioning, business development, and strategic firm initiatives. From thought leadership and executive communications to digital content, campaign copy, and stories about our work and people, you’ll help ensure everything we publish is clear, credible, and unmistakably HGA. The ideal candidate is both a strong writer and a disciplined editor—someone who can generate original ideas, collaborate with subject matter experts, and maintain a consistent brand voice across diverse formats. Curiosity, strategic thinking, and audience awareness are essential, as is the ability to translate complex concepts from architecture, engineering, planning, interiors, research, and innovation into engaging, accessible narratives. You'll thrive at HGA if you ... Are energized by turning complex ideas into clear, compelling stories Can shape and refine content without losing the original voice behind it Have a strong instinct for tone, voice, and narrative Stay organized and thoughtful in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment Enjoy connecting storytelling to strategy, brand, and business goals In this role you will: Thought Leadership & Storytelling Develop and write multi-format content including articles, insights, project stories, executive perspectives, video scripts, and campaign narratives. Translate complex design, technical, and market-specific ideas into clear, compelling, and commercially relevant messaging. Identify and shape timely themes across HGA's eight markets to drive proactive storytelling. Partner with firm leaders to elevate executive voice and external visibility. Editorial Excellence & Brand Voice Serve as a steward of HGA's brand voice, ensuring consistency, clarity, and quality across all content. Edit and refine content from subject matter experts and contributors across the firm. Establish and evolve editorial standards, frameworks, and storytelling approaches. Champion the audience perspective, ensuring content is engaging, relevant, and easy to understand. Content Strategy & Campaign Integration Collaborate with digital, PR, and marketing teams to develop integrated content across campaigns and channels. Adapt and repurpose content for web, social, email, video, and presentations. Contribute to editorial calendars aligned to market priorities and firm initiatives. Support strategic initiatives including brand evolution, website experience, and awareness campaigns. Collaboration & Workflow Partner cross-functionally with market sector leaders, designers, digital strategists, and PR Lead or support content development workflows (briefs, interviews, outlines, reviews) Ensure alignment across stakeholders while maintaining efficiency and momentum Utilize tools and platforms (e.g., CMS, HubSpot, DAM) to manage and publish content Performance & Optimization Leverage insights, analytics, and feedback to continuously improve content effectiveness. Identify opportunities for new content based on performance, trends, and business needs. Contribute to building a scalable, measurable content ecosystem. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in English, Journalism, Communications, Marketing, or a related field, or equivalent experience. 8-10+ years of experience in editorial, content strategy, or brand writing, ideally in professional services, AEC, or similarly complex industries. Exceptional writing and editing skills, with the ability to move seamlessly between big-picture narrative and detail-oriented execution. Proven ability to translate complex concepts into clear, engaging content. Experience developing thought leadership and executive-level communications. Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills across diverse teams. Comfort working across formats: long-form, short-form, digital, video, and social. A strategic mindset with the ability to connect content to business goals and audience needs. Familiarity with digital platforms, content systems, and performance metrics. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $104,100 - 123,300 (Mid-West) or $110,800 - $133,200 (East and West Coast) depending on qualifications and experience. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects and engineers—and help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

title: Sr Public Relations Manager
description: Shape the Stories Behind Award-Winning Design – Join HGA as a Senior Public Relations Manager ! As a national leader in architecture, engineering, and design, HGA is recognized not only for the places we create—but for the ideas, insights, and impact that shape them. The Senior PR Manager is central to amplifying that voice on a national stage. In this role, you will lead the development and execution of HGA’s public relations strategy, elevating our brand, strengthening our market presence, and positioning our people, projects, and perspectives at the forefront of industry and cultural conversations. You’ll collaborate closely with firm leadership, marketing teams, and external partners to drive integrated, high‑impact visibility across markets and channels. This position reflects an expanded, modern vision of PR—one that goes beyond traditional media relations to build credibility, discoverability, and influence. You will partner directly with clients to amplify shared stories, extend HGA’s reach through their platforms and audiences, and navigate an evolving landscape shaped by AI, search, and cross‑channel content ecosystems. You'll t hrive  at HGA if you... Think beyond traditional PR and are excited to shape how a brand shows up across channels, platforms, and partnerships Are energized by turning complex ideas into clear, compelling stories that resonate with multiple audiences Can operate at both a strategic and executional level—connecting big-picture thinking to day-to-day action Build strong relationships easily, from journalists to clients to internal leaders Are curious about how media, content, and technology are evolving—and eager to adapt and lead through that change Enjoy working across disciplines and bringing people together around a shared narrative and purpose In this role you will: Strategic Leadership & Planning Lead the development and execution of HGA’s firmwide PR strategy, aligned with brand, marketing, and business priorities. Define and oversee annual PR plans and integrated campaigns across markets and sectors. Establish KPIs and measurement frameworks to track brand awareness, media impact, and campaign performance. Monitor and translate emerging trends (media, AI/search, content distribution) into actionable strategy. Partner with firm leadership to shape narratives and elevate executive visibility. Media Relations & Campaign Execution Oversee proactive and reactive media strategy across national, regional, and trade outlets. Build and maintain strong relationships with key journalists, editors, and industry influencers. Lead development of press materials, messaging frameworks, and story pitches. Drive high-impact PR campaigns tied to firm initiatives, priority projects, and market growth areas. Client Partnership & External Amplification Partner with clients as an extension of their teams to shape and promote shared project stories and impact. Identify and execute co-marketing and cross-promotion opportunities that elevate both HGA and client brands. Expand HGA's presence through client channels, industry platforms, and strategic partnerships. Contribute to the evolution of PR as a value-added, client-facing service. Team Leadership & Collaboration Help build and scale a high-performing PR function within Brand & Communications. Manage external agencies and partners to ensure alignment, quality, and performance. Collaborate across marketing, business development, and operations to align messaging and maximize impact. Mentor and support team members, fostering growth, clarity, and strong execution. What you will need to have: 12–15+ years of experience in public relations, strategic communications, or integrated marketing (agency and/or in-house). Proven experience leading PR strategy and securing high-impact earned media at national and industry levels. Strong leadership presence with experience advising executives and influencing senior stakeholders. Exceptional storytelling and writing skills, with the ability to translate complex ideas into clear, compelling narratives. Experience managing integrated campaigns across PR, content, and digital channels. Strong understanding of the evolving media landscape, including the impact of AI and search on visibility and content distribution. Experience working in professional services, AEC, or a related industry preferred. Familiarity with PR and analytics tools (e.g., Meltwater, Cision, Muck Rack). Highly collaborative, proactive, and strategic thinker with strong attention to detail. Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment while managing multiple priorities. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $138,800 - $153,700 (Mid-West) or $145,300 - $162,700 (East and West Coast) depending on qualifications and experience. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects and engineers—and help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

description: JOB DESCRIPTION: GIỚI THIỆU VỀ ABBOTT Abbott là công ty chăm sóc sức khỏe toàn cầu, tiên phong trong nghiên cứu khoa học và tạo ra những đột phá trong công nghệ chăm sóc sức khỏe để giúp mọi người sống khỏe mạnh hơn. Chúng tôi luôn hướng tới tương lai, dự đoán và đáp ứng những thay đổi trong khoa học và công nghệ y khoa. Những công nghệ mang tính thay đổi cuộc sống của Abbott trải dài nhiều lĩnh vực từ dinh dưỡng, chẩn đoán đến thiết bị y tế và dược phẩm. LÀM VIỆC TẠI ABBOTT Tại Abbott, bạn có thể làm công việc tạo ra giá trị và ý nghĩa cho cộng đồng. Bạn được trao cơ hội để học hỏi và phát triển, chăm sóc bản thân và gia đình, đồng thời thể hiện và phát huy tối đa tiềm năng của bản thân trong cả sự nghiệp và cuộc sống. Bạn sẽ có cơ hội: Phát triển sự nghiệp tại một công ty toàn cầu, nơi bạn có thể hiện thực hóa ước mơ nghề nghiệp của mình. Làm việc tại một môi trường được công nhận là “Nơi làm việc tốt nhất” tại nhiều quốc gia và được tạp chí Fortune (Mỹ) vinh danh là một trong những công ty được ngưỡng mộ nhất thế giới. Trở thành một phần của doanh nghiệp hàng đầu, được công nhận là nơi làm việc tốt nhất với môi trường tuyệt vời cho sự đa dạng và hòa nhập. Vận hành gian hàng (Shop Operation) Theo dõi và kiểm soát các chỉ số vận hành shop Trả lời tin nhắn, chăm sóc khách hàng Xử lý khiếu nại để đảm bảo xếp hạng và hình ảnh shop Phối hợp với đối tác vận chuyển & bộ phận fulfillment để xử lý các vấn đề phát sinh YÊU CẦU Kinh nghiệm Tối thiểu 1 năm kinh nghiệm ở vị trí tương đương hoặc có liên quan Có kinh nghiệm vận hành nền tảng TMĐT / TikTok Shop là lợi thế Học vấn Tốt nghiệp Đại học Kỹ năng Kỹ năng vận hành chi tiết, phân tích số liệu Khả năng sáng tạo nội dung Làm việc nhóm tốt, phối hợp đa phòng ban Chủ động, linh hoạt, có tinh thần trách nhiệm Tiếng Anh cơ bản Sử dụng Excel, PowerPoint ở mức cơ bản The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
description: This position is working 40 hours per week, any 5 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour(£13.45 per hour from 1 May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

description: Technical Manager – Be at the Heart of a Major New Investment Join apetito at a transformational moment in our journey. Having exceeded £260m turnover, we are now investing over £100m in building a state‑of‑the‑art manufacturing facility —a major milestone that will support our continued growth and innovation in food manufacturing. As our new Technical Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role not only in leading food safety and quality during the operational phase, but also in shaping the future of the site as it comes to life. This includes valuable involvement throughout the installation and commissioning of the new facility—an opportunity few roles can offer. What You’ll Be Doing While construction is underway, you’ll be based in our existing kitchen, located just a short walk from the new site. Reporting to the Group Technical Manager, you will: Work closely with the current Site Technical Manager to support the team with a strong focus on systems, governance, and compliance Build a deep understanding of our complex operation—managing hundreds of ingredients and materials that go into producing hundreds of delicious, nutritious recipes Begin partnering with the project team as the new build progresses, ensuring that food safety and quality standards are fully embedded throughout installation and commissioning Recruit, develop, and inspire your new team, building strong relationships across the site Design and implement robust quality systems to support the new kitchen’s launch Ensure we achieve food registration and secure an AA grade against the BRCGS Global Standard for Food Safety at the earliest opportunity

title: Customer Service Advisor
description: This role is working 30 hours per week, any 4 in 6 days The role of our Customer Services Advisor is critical to the success of our business. Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. The defining characteristics of our Customer Services Advisors are that they’re organised and efficient. However, apart from the technical skills that are required with the role, the most important attributes are that we demonstrate empathy, patience, kindness, friendliness, care and warmth to all our customers. The role involves the taking of customer orders and also the making of outbound courtesy calls. These calls are particularly important as many customers live alone and the phone calls they receive are often the only contact they may have in a single day. As such, many customers become very attached to their “friends” at Wiltshire Farm Foods. Wiltshire Farm Foods supplies frozen meal solutions direct to the consumer. We have a cold store to accommodate the stock necessary to be able to be able to deliver to our customers each week. The ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceed expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

title: Registered Nurse (RN), Variable Part-Time Day Shift,  PACU
description: Shady Grove Medical Center If you are a current Adventist HealthCare employee, please click this link to apply through your Workday account. Shady Grove Medical Center seeks to hire a Registered Nurse who will embrace our mission to extend God’s care through the ministry of physical, mental and spiritual healing. As a PACU Registered Nurse, you will: • Educate patients and family through the continuum of care while also striving to advance their own knowledge and skills through careful consideration of feedback and guidance provided by more senior nurses and the Clinical Nurse Manager. • Serve as role models to Level I nurses and use electronic resources to support best practices in patient care. • Demonstrate professionalism, adheres to ANA standards of practice, and exemplifies the Adventist HealthCare values as they fulfill our nursing mission and vision. • Use equipment and technology correctly, to delegate non-nursing duties appropriately, and to maximize efficiency and effectiveness • Develop a plan of care according to patient population. • Identify impending changes in patient condition accurately and initiates corrective action. • Mentor students, colleagues and team members in patient-centered care. • Conduct purposeful rounding from patient and family perspective. • Conduct efficient literature searches and applies findings appropriately to practice. • Identify opportunities for improvement in clinical care and participates in change of practice based on evidence. Qualifications include: • Graduate of an accredited nursing program • Passing score on NCLEX • Minimum 1 year of RN experience required. PACU Registered Nurse experience or related acute care experience preferred • Current Maryland State nursing license • Active American Heart Association Basic Life Support (BLS) certification required • Active American Heart Association (ACLS) certification required • Active American Heart Association (PALS) certification required Work Schedule: Employment Type: Variable Part-time Hours per Week: 8 hrs/week Typical Daily Schedule: Varied hours Shift Type: Day Weekend Requirements: no Holiday Requirements: Required/On-call/Follow company holiday calendar On-Call: no Shift Differential Eligible: Yes Pay Range: $37.00 - $55.00 If the salary range is not displayed or if the position is Per Diem (with a fixed rate), salary discussions will take place during the screening process. Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), this position is classified as: United States of America (Non-Exempt) At Adventist HealthCare our job is to care for you. We do this by offering: Work life balance through nonrotating shifts Recognition and rewards for professional expertise 403(b) retirement plan Free Employee parking Employee Assistance Program (EAP) support As a faith-based organization, with over a century of caring for the communities in the Maryland area, Adventist HealthCare has earned a reputation for high-quality, compassionate care. Adventist HealthCare was the first and is the largest healthcare provider in Montgomery County. If you want to make a difference in someone’s life every day, consider a position with a team of professionals who are doing just that, making a difference. Join the Adventist HealthCare team today, apply now to be considered! COVID-19 Vaccination Adventist HealthCare strongly recommends all applicants to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 before commencing employment. Applicants may be required to furnish proof of vaccination. Tobacco and Drug Statement Tobacco use is a well-recognized preventable cause of death in the United States and an important public health issue. In order to promote and maintain a healthy work environment, Adventist HealthCare will not hire applicants for employment who either state that they are nicotine users or who test positive for nicotine and drug use. While some jurisdictions, including Maryland, permit the use of marijuana for medical purposes, marijuana continues to be classified as an illegal drug under the federal Controlled Substances Act.  As a result, medical marijuana use will not be accepted as a valid explanation for a positive drug test result. Adventist HealthCare will withdraw offers of employment to applicants who test positive for Cotinine (nicotine) and marijuana. Those testing positive are given the opportunity to re-apply in 90 days, if they can truthfully attest that they have not used any nicotine products in the past ninety (90) days and successfully pass follow-up testing. ("Nicotine products" include, but are not limited to: cigarettes, cigars, pipes, chewing tobacco, e-cigarettes, vaping products, hookah, and nicotine replacement products (e.g., nicotine gum, nicotine patches, nicotine lozenges, etc.). Equal Employment Opportunity Adventist HealthCare is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We are committed to attracting, engaging, and developing the best people to cultivate our mission-centric culture. Our goal is to have a welcoming, equitable, and safe place to work and grow for all employees, no matter their background. AHC does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy and related medical conditions, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Adventist HealthCare will make reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities, in accordance with applicable law.  Adventist HealthCare is a religious organization as defined under applicable law; however, it will endeavor to provide reasonable accommodations for applicants’ religious beliefs. Applicants who wish to request accommodations for disabilities or religious belief should contact the Support Center HR Office.

title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
description: This position is working 24 hours per week, any 3 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

description: Shape the future of healthcare design with HGA! Project Architect – Healthcare in Sacramento, San Francisco, Santa Monica or San Diego, CA HGA, a leading architectural, engineering, and planning firm, is seeking a talented and driven Project Architect to join our growing healthcare practice group in Northern and Southern California. We’re looking for someone with strong communication, presentation, time management, and interpersonal skills who thrives in a collaborative environment and is passionate about advancing healthcare design. In this role, you’ll work closely with Senior Designers, Project Managers, and other Project Architects to develop innovative and technically sound design solutions. You’ll lead the creation of detailed documentation while maintaining our firm’s high standards for design quality and client service. What You’ll Do: Lead the development of construction documents and ensure design intent is accurately carried through. Translate complex project goals into creative and functional design solutions. Collaborate across disciplines to coordinate architectural and engineering systems. Participate in client meetings and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders. Ensure project milestones and deadlines are met with attention to detail and quality. Why HGA? Collaborative Culture: be part of a firm known for its integrated, cross-disciplinary teamwork and strong design ethos; Professional Growth: access to mentorship, continuing education, and leadership opportunities; and Meaningful Work: contribute to impactful healthcare projects that improve patient outcomes and shape the future of care in Calfornia. In this role you will: Design and u nderstand and contribute to the project's design philosophy. Provide major input in developing design and building performance strategies and convey these effectively to team members. Lead project teams to develop complete document sets based on the project phase. Provide technical design input and solutions to project challenges, supporting the overall design concept. Oversee technical design efforts, detailed design development, project coordination, and quality control during all project phases, collaborating closely with the Senior Designer. Execute applicable agency review analysis (accessibility, zoning, life-safety, etc.). Actively collaborate with and coordinate the work of consultants and knowledge resources to ensure project deliverables are coordinated, integrated, and support the design concept and performance goals. Set an example for team effort through the spirit of cooperation while directing, organizing, and mentoring junior staff. C ontribute to the preparation of design documents and lead the detailed development of construction documents during all project phases. Ensure complete, accurate, and coordinated project documentation. Oversee and perform construction administration duties, including reviewing shop drawings, material samples, RFI’s, RFP’s, and change orders for conformance with design. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in Architecture California Architectural Licensure required LEED accreditation preferred 10+ years of experience, including work on small to large-scale projects. Understanding of the healthcare industry, especially in areas of clinical innovation, operational improvement, planning, and strategic positioning for complex hospital systems Deep understanding of healthcare programming, codes, and technical detailing Strong leadership and collaboration skills in team environments Proficiency in Revit required; experience with AutoCAD, SketchUp, Enscape, and other graphic tools desired. Expertise with project management software and Microsoft Office Suite products needed Excellent communication, presentation, and time management skills Experience with Lean and Evidence-based design is a plus. Experience with alternative delivery methods (IPD, Design Build, IFOA) preferred Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $108,200 - $130,000 depending on location (SAC, SF, LA or SD), qualifications and experience. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects, engineers, and interior designers — help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume and portfolio for consideration.

title: Project Manager
description: Join HGA and learn what passion for design really means in spaces that touch people's lives! Locations: Sacramento, San Fransisco, Santa Monica, or San Diego, CA Bring your experience and design expertise to an innovative architecture, engineering and interior design firm dedicated to designing exceptional spaces that inspire and transform communities. We are looking for a talented, design-sensitive, and ambitious Project Manager to join our California healthcare architecture teams and help shape the future of healthcare design. In this role, you will be an integral member of our project teams, contributing at every stage of the design process. Our practice encompasses a broad array of project types and scales, so versatility and adaptability to various collaborative design team settings are essential. Why HGA? Collaborative Culture: be part of a firm known for its integrated, cross-disciplinary teamwork and strong design ethos; Professional Growth: access to mentorship, continuing education, and leadership opportunities; and Meaningful Work: contribute to impactful projects that improve and shape the future architectural design. In this role you will: Collaborate with Principal in evaluation of project requirements and help to negotiate an acceptable owner-architect agreement including the fee, scope, and time schedule. Demonstrate task-focused mastery of project scope, schedule, budget and profitability. Complete projects on schedule, within budget and with maximum client satisfaction. Manage a program of multiple projects and associated client relationships effectively. Negotiate critical issues with Owner and Contractor as well as Architects, Engineers and Specialty Consultants within HGA and of other organizations and firms. Supervise the production of architectural construction documents for multiple small renovation projects. Lead a full-service architectural and engineering team, using past experience and technical knowledge to efficiently deliver quality documents, specifications, reports and cost estimates, keeping in mind schedules/budgets and provides coaching/communication as appropriate with team leaders and/or team members who are responsible for work flow/timelines, etc. Develop discipline cost and work-hour budgets for each project and monitor project costs, completion status against budget schedules/timelines. Oversee all correspondence and pertinent documents, making sure they are accurate; sign or obtain signatures and seek legal counsel as appropriate. Coordinate review and evaluation of contractor bids and work with Principal so that award recommendation can be submitted to owner. Provide day-to-day leadership in the absence of and as delegated by the Principal and/or Practice Group Leader keeping lines of communication open with all office management leaders and assist with preparation of client billings prior to submittal for signature. Collaborate with Practice Group Leader as requested in the marketing of projects including development of proposals and possible participation in client interviews. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in architecture or related field Licensed professional preferred 8-10 years of project management experience honed on increasingly more complex projects Demonstrated skill in working on multiple smaller-sized to mid-sized, challenging projects simultaneously Exceptional interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate clearly with team members and clients and lead discussions on project budgets and management of project delivery Proficiency with industry accepted software and Microsoft Office products is needed, experience with Project Management software (Vision, Microsoft Project) is beneficial Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. title: RTR Manager
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: ABOUT ABBOTT Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritional’s and branded generic medicines. Our 114,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. ABBOTT IRELAND Abbott serves the Irish market with a diverse range of health care products including diagnostics, medical devices and nutritional products. In Ireland, Abbott employs almost 6,000 people across ten sites. We have six manufacturing facilities located in Clonmel, Cootehill, Donegal, Kilkenny, Longford and Sligo and a third-party manufacturing management operation in Sligo. Abbott has commercial, support operations and shared services in Dublin and Galway. We have been operating in Ireland since 1946. We currently have a vacancy for a Manager within our Record to Report section of the Shared Services team, supporting the Netherlands market. The successful candidate will join the Shared Services Centre based either in Cherrywood Business Park, Loughlinstown, Co Dublin or Ballybrit Business Park in Galway KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: Leads an accounting team, performing general accounting processes from the point of transaction through to financial close and periodic external reporting (monthly, quarterly, and annually) in support of corporate, country/legal entity and regional controllers Responsible for Month end close process, close governance, associated corporate reporting, and ensuring prompt response to Internal/ External Audit requests for in scope countries Support the advancement of the Target Operating Model (TOM) by identifying additional activities to be transitioned to the outsource partner(s), while continuing to transition more complex activities into the CFS EMEA Hub Report on agreed performance measures, KPIs, and goal setting processes to support Shared Services Support and guide the internal and external teams to identify, implement and maintain effective controls for the finance process activities performed to ensure ongoing data and financial integrity for Abbott Support the continued development of team members utilizing Abbott’s training and development tools Manage the delivery of accounting activities provided by our 3rd party BPO provider for in scope countries Lead the team on ad hoc and special projects assignments, including system migrations and embed a continuous improvement mindset within the team Sustain Service Agreement delivery standards with the internal customers and 3rd party outsource service providers. KEY REQUIREMENTS : Accounting Qualification required (ACCA/CIMA/ACA/CPA) BSc in Accounting or Finance preferred 5+ years relevant experience in a complex global environment – preferably within Shared Services servicing a multi-currency, US GAAP based financial close 3-5 years team lead experience, mentoring and developing teams Experience working with working with 3rd party service providers essential Highly Motivated with strong communication, technical, analytical, and problem-solving skills. Ability to work and collaborate with others, building/leveraging relationships across functions and organizations to achieve business results. Continuous improvement mindset and proven track record in delivering projects Language Skills:  English required, other languages desirable Preferred Experience Audit experience preferred Experience in development, implementation and optimisation of Hybrid Target Operation Models to meet changing business needs The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. title: Manager de l’équipe Plateform & Databrain (F/H)
description: Dans le cadre du développement rapide de notre service Xtended, nous recherchons un manager pour mettre en place et piloter les activités expertise, plateforme et IA. Vous encadrerez une équipe en charge des architectures solution, de la plateforme de production et de la mise en place de l’intelligence artificielle dans le cadre du service. Vos principales missions seront : Incarner notre offre technologique Comprendre le marché de la détection et réponse, ses enjeux et ses tendances Elaborer une vision et contribuer à la construction de notre proposition de valeur Contribuer aux phases d’avant-vente avec : La mise en avant de notre approche technologique L’accompagnement de nos clients sur ses orientations stratégiques Piloter l’activité Professional Service Encadrer l’équipe et assurer sa montée en maturité sur les différentes technologies, l’architecture sécurité au sens large et sa capacité à prendre du recul Piloter le bon déroulement des activités de production avec : Le cadrage technique des dossiers en phase avant-vente La définition des architectures techniques en phase de build Le support niveau 3 expertise en phase de run Accélérer le développement de l’approche plateforme par l’incubation des différents modules et leur mise en service dans le cadre du service Pilotage de l’activité plateforme Définir une feuille de route plateforme alignée à nos enjeux présents et futurs Orchestrer la mise en œuvre de la feuille de route plateforme Assurer la gestion quotidienne de la plateforme Piloter l’activité data et intelligence artificielle Elaborer une feuille de route de mise en œuvre de l’IA au sein du service Assurer la mise en œuvre de la feuille de route des proof of value jusqu’à la production S’assurer du déploiement de Qevlar.ai dans le cadre du service de détection et réponse Manager les différentes équipes Participer à la définition des profils recherchés, rédiger les offres d’emploi, conduire les entretiens de sélection, et intégrer les nouveaux membres de l’équipe. Gestion de la capacité et identification des besoins en termes de recrutement Organiser et réaliser des entretiens individuels réguliers pour faire le point sur les missions, évaluer la performance et fixer les objectifs. Identifier les besoins en formation, encourager la montée en compétences, et favoriser un environnement d’apprentissage continu. Maintenir un climat de travail positif, encourager la collaboration, et gérer les éventuels conflits. Assurer la gestion administrative liée à l’équipe (planning, absences, congés, etc.)

title: Orthopedic and Spine OR First Assistant
Plumbing Designer
description: Plumbing Designer - Washington, DC Join HGA—an award-winning architecture, engineering, interior design, and planning firm—where your technical skills and creative thinking can make a national impact. We’re seeking a Plumbing Designer to join our growing Mechanical team. At HGA, you’ll collaborate with a multidisciplinary group of architects, engineers, and designers on meaningful projects spanning healthcare, science & technology, cultural institutions, corporate campuses, and infrastructure . Every project is an opportunity to shape the built environment using cutting-edge tools and innovative thinking . What You'll Do: Design plumbing systems including domestic water, sanitary waste/vent, medical gas, storm drainage, and specialty systems. Collaborate with team members across disciplines from concept through construction. Contribute to technical documentation, specifications, and code compliance. Leverage BIM/Revit and other digital tools to develop coordinated designs. If you're ready to bring your passion to impactful, high-performance designs that shape communities nationwide—we want to hear from you. In this role you will: Be responsible for plumbing design including calculating building loads for all plumbing systems, select equipment, layout piping, create and edit project details, and write and edit project engineering specifications. Participate in design review meetings and workshops. Support a variety of complex design challenges including conflicting Owner and project requirements, application of different system types, or challenging field conditions. Research design options and document findings for the project team; Assure drawings and designs are in accordance with local codes and design standards. Assist with coordination with architectural and other engineering disciplines as applicable. You will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in architectural engineering, mechanical engineering or related field desired. Up to 2 years of consulting engineering experience with emphasis on plumbing design for healthcare, commercial, municipal, institutional and government facilities. Knowledge of applicable codes and regulations, standard engineering practices, techniques, and procedures. Excellent problem-solving skills. Ability to work on several projects simultaneously and prioritize work. Excellent communication skills. Positive attitude, team player with a collaborative approach. Interest in professional growth and mentoring. Revit, BIM, and Microsoft Office proficiency required. Professional Engineer or Certification recognized by a state licensing board desired. Knowledge in HVAC systems and building controls preferred, but not required. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted . Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $42.42/hr (new graduate rate) - $46.42/hr depending on qualifications and experience . Additional Information: HGA is a place where you can do your best work. We believe that enduring, impactful design results from deep insight into the people and passions that animate each unique environment. We value empathy, are fueled by curiosity, and embrace the hard work that leads to innovation. Equally important is our commitment to creating a culture of inclusivity and diversity where we encourage your originality, respect the need for flexibility and work-life balance, and empower your desired career growth. HGA is a national award-winning interdisciplinary design firm rooted in architecture and engineering. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

title: Pharmacy Tech (Tech I), 6-Week Training Program, Rotating Shift, Pharmacy
description: Shady Grove Medical Center If you are a current Adventist HealthCare employee, please click this link to apply through your Workday account. Shady Grove Medical Center seeks to hire an experienced Pharmacy Technician for our Pharmacy department who will embrace our mission to extend God’s care through the ministry of physical, mental, and spiritual healing. As a Pharmacy Technician you will: • Prepare and deliver medication to patient care units and restock automated dispensing units • Maintain accurate inventory of medications and supplies by performing inventory management cycle counts and perform patient care unit inspections with electronic documentation • Assess need for and repackage oral solid and liquid medication for dispensing • Demonstrate a positive attitude on patient safety, lifelong learning, professionalism, and customer experience • Perform additional duties as assigned Qualifications include: • High school diploma or GED/equivalent • Required: Licensure as Pharmacy Technician by the Maryland Board of Pharmacy • Strongly Preferred: 1 year of pharmacy experience • Preferred: Previous experience with electronic medical records (e.g. Cerner, EPIC), automated medication management (Pyxis, Omnicell, BD Logistics) Work Schedule: Work Schedule: This 6-week training program is designed to introduce Pharmacy Technicians to hospital based practice and will be a combination of clinical and technical competence including: Classroom Lecture On-Site Training Unit dose training IV Room training Supervised shifts Final exam and performance review & evaluation Location & Schedule: Weeks 1 & 2 at Adventist HealthCare Support Center in Gaithersburg, MD Weeks 3-6 at Shady Grove Medical Center, Rockville, MD Training program is a Monday - Friday 8 hour shift from 9am-5pm **Post 6-week training program, the position becomes a standard full-time rotating shift position. Upon hire you will complete a six-week Pharmacy Technician Training Program, to develop clinical and technical competence in health-system pharmacy through a variety of modalities, including classroom, simulation and application of learning in a hospital pharmacy setting. Demonstrated competence will include clinical skills (medication knowledge and pharmacy calculations), technical skills (Cerner, Logistics and Pyxis), and interpersonal skills (building relationships with other healthcare providers, patients, and team members). Pay Range: $19.64 - $25.65 If the salary range is listed as $0 or if the position is Per Diem (with a fixed rate), salary discussions will take place during the screening process. Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), this position is classified as: United States of America (Non-Exempt) At Adventist HealthCare our job is to care for you. We do this by offering: Work life balance through nonrotating shifts Recognition and rewards for professional expertise Free Employee parking Medical, Prescription, Dental, and Vision coverage for employees and their eligible dependents effective on your date of hire Employer-paid Short & Long-Term Disability, Basic Life Insurance and AD&D, (short-term disability buy-up available) Paid Time Off Employer retirement contribution and match after 1-year of eligible employment with a 3-year vesting period Voluntary benefits include flexible spending accounts, legal plans, and life, pet, auto, home, long term care, and critical illness & accident insurance Subsidized childcare at participating childcare centers Tuition Reimbursement Employee Assistance Program (EAP) support As a faith-based organization, with over a century of caring for the communities in the Maryland area, Adventist HealthCare has earned a reputation for high-quality, compassionate care. Adventist HealthCare was the first and is the largest healthcare provider in Montgomery County. If you want to make a difference in someone’s life every day, consider a position with a team of professionals who are doing just that, making a difference. Join the Adventist HealthCare team today, apply now to be considered! COVID-19 Vaccination Adventist HealthCare strongly recommends all applicants to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 before commencing employment. Applicants may be required to furnish proof of vaccination. Tobacco and Drug Statement Tobacco use is a well-recognized preventable cause of death in the United States and an important public health issue. In order to promote and maintain a healthy work environment, Adventist HealthCare will not hire applicants for employment who either state that they are nicotine users or who test positive for nicotine and drug use. While some jurisdictions, including Maryland, permit the use of marijuana for medical purposes, marijuana continues to be classified as an illegal drug under the federal Controlled Substances Act.  As a result, medical marijuana use will not be accepted as a valid explanation for a positive drug test result. Adventist HealthCare will withdraw offers of employment to applicants who test positive for Cotinine (nicotine) and marijuana. Those testing positive are given the opportunity to re-apply in 90 days, if they can truthfully attest that they have not used any nicotine products in the past ninety (90) days and successfully pass follow-up testing. ("Nicotine products" include, but are not limited to: cigarettes, cigars, pipes, chewing tobacco, e-cigarettes, vaping products, hookah, and nicotine replacement products (e.g., nicotine gum, nicotine patches, nicotine lozenges, etc.). Equal Employment Opportunity Adventist HealthCare is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We are committed to attracting, engaging, and developing the best people to cultivate our mission-centric culture. Our goal is to have a welcoming, equitable, and safe place to work and grow for all employees, no matter their background. AHC does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy and related medical conditions, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Adventist HealthCare will make reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities, in accordance with applicable law.  Adventist HealthCare is a religious organization as defined under applicable law; however, it will endeavor to provide reasonable accommodations for applicants’ religious beliefs. Applicants who wish to request accommodations for disabilities or religious belief should contact the Support Center HR Office.

Initiate and develop detailed design concepts, with a strong ability to meet functional and budgetary requirements and established project-design criteria. title: Senior TA Manager
description: JOB DESCRIPTION: ABOUT ABBOTT Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritional and branded generic medicines. Our 114,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. As Senior Talent Acquisition Manager, you will play a critical role in setting the strategy and leading a team of internal recruiters and external partnerships to effectively attract, hire, and maintain a continuous pipeline of well-qualified talent. Working at Abbott At Abbott, you can do work that matters, grow, and learn, care for yourself and family, be your true self and live a full life. You will have access to: Career development with an international company where you can grow the career you dream of. A company recognized as a great place to work in dozens of countries around the world and named one of the most admired companies in the world by Fortune. A company that is recognized as one of the best big companies to work for as well as a best place to work for diversity, working mothers, female executives, and scientists. ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Main purpose of position: This role will lead the talent acquisition strategy and hiring operations for Abbott Nutrition & Corporate Functions which includes talent acquisition support for all business and functional teams. Scope of role will ensure standardization of talent recruitment policies, procedures, practices, tools, & metrics, as well as deliver on the hiring requirements from business. Major areas of responsibility include developing and executing the recruiting strategies and solutions to meet/exceed the business staffing requirements, team management, stakeholder management. 2. Main responsibilities: Provide leadership and direction to a team of TA professionals and ensure Abbott attracts high caliber talent to meet business needs. Foster a continuous learning environment that champions a diverse and equitable culture. Support and lead project work by driving and executing against key expansion plans or functional projects and support local, regional PRM initiatives as appropriate. Establish the most effective recruitment processes and selection practices in. Execute with high quality hiring standards to meet diversity goals and provide exceptional candidate experiences . Own and manage the relationship with internal clients at senior manager, director, GM, occasionally up to DVP level . Deliver full cycle recruiting efforts and activities, from requirement definition to negotiation for leadership and most difficult to hire positions . May use the additional support of a sourcing specialist or a contingency search partner as required. Use cost-effective recruiting channels, implement direct sourcing initiatives, and create innovative campaigns to attract the best talent. MINIMUM BACKGROUND/ EXPERIENCE REQUIRED Work Experience : Minimum professional experience: 10 + years of recruiting experience with a min 3 years of in-house TA experience in a matrix environment. People management experience preferred. Functional experience: Full cycle recruitment experience in healthcare or adjacent industries and across main functions: commercial, scientific, and corporate function. Experience of supporting hiring needs at senior manager/director level across multiple functions, divisions or business units. Subject matter expert in developing marketing campaigns and direct sourcing strategies to engage, attract, and nurture passive candidates. Team leadership experience over a single contributor team or indirect leadership over resources assigned to a project and acquired in a matrix environment. Critical capabilities: Strategy development and execution Influence stakeholders and candidates Endurance and resilience Leadership Change management Data management, analysis and insights, story-telling Project management Language Skill: Advanced written and spoken English proficiency Vietnamese Computer Skill: Able to utilize MS computer programs such as notes, excel and PowerPoint to compile, build, analyses and consolidate data. MINIMUM EDUCATION REQUIRED Bachelor's Degree or equivalent combination of education and work experience The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. description: Pediatric Cardiac Intensivist MemorialCare Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach MemorialCare Medical Group Base Salary Range*: $300,000 to $310,000 *The salary range above is an estimated annual base salary for a full-time position. Your Total Rewards would include a base salary plus performance-based incentives and other valuable benefits. Our compensation models utilize fair market value data specific to your specialty. Memorial Care Medical Group (MCMG) is looking for a Pediatric Cardiac Intensivist for our dedicated Pediatric Cardiothoracic Intensive Care Unit (CTICU) at Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach (MCWHLB). The new state of the art 8-bed CTICU and an additional 6-bed stepdown unit are part of our Heart Institute’s rapid growth to serve the needs of the southern California community. Candidates with strong clinical and teaching skills are encouraged to apply. The candidate should be board-certified in Pediatric Critical Care plus advanced fellowship training in Pediatric Cardiac Critical Care or a record of excellence in the clinical care of congenital cardiac patients. The successful candidate will work closely with the CTICU Medical Director Dr. Constantinos Chrysostomou and a multidisciplinary team of pediatric cardiac intensivists, cardiac surgeons, cardiologists, dedicated CTICU nurses and other specialists to further develop a state-of-the-art clinical program. Successful candidates should have (or be eligible for) an unrestricted California medical license. As part of the Memorial Care Medical Group, you will be given the tools you need to build the career you want, and the flexibility to adapt as your personal needs and professional interests change. We invite you to grow with us and help shape the future of this community. Robust compensation and benefits package are provided. Memorial Care Miller Children’s & Women’s Hospital of Long Beach, is a private, not for profit children’s hospital first opened in 1970. It is a 324 bed children’s hospital that treats more than 8,000 children each year and has become a regional pediatric destination for more than 84,000 children. It has an active perinatal and maternal fetal medicine service with over 7000 deliveries annually to the adjacent birth center and one of the largest Neonatal Intensive Care units (NICU) in the area with over 90 beds. The hospital serves as the major referral center for the over 500,000 people of Long Beach, but reaches an additional 2 million people in the immediate vicinity. The hospital has an academic affiliation with the University of California Los Angeles (UCLA) and the University of California Irvine (UCI) to which the successful candidate is eligible to apply for a faculty position. Financial & Benefits • Employed position with MemorialCare Medical Group • Competitive salary with incentive compensation • Eligible to be considered for Shareholder/Partner status with MCMG after two years of full-time employment • Full and comprehensive benefits for Physician and family • 401(k) retirement plan with employer matching • 529 College Savings Plan About Long Beach and Southern California Located on the coast of the Pacific Ocean south of Los Angeles and just west of Orange County, Long Beach is the sixth largest city in the State of California. Offering all the world class amenities of a large metropolitan city, coupled with its strong sense of community and pride, Long Beach is one of the most vibrant communities in the country. The city has been referred to as the "Most Diverse City" in the country by USA Today and one of the "Best Communities for Young People" two years in a row. With its ideal location in Southern California, year-round comfortable climate, healthy business environment and far ranging cultural pursuits, the city is alive with activity. Long Beach is home to an abundance of cultural and recreational options. Expansive beaches, three marinas, five golf courses, the Aquarium of the Pacific, the Queen Mary and the annual Grand Prix of Long Beach serve to draw 6.5 million visitors a year. Long Beach has easy access to all the amenities and attractions of Los Angeles, Orange, and San Bernardino counties, including professional sporting events, music festivals, gourmet dining, film and television studios, Disneyland, and snow skiing in the local mountains.

title: Physician, Aortic Surgery Fellow
description: Base Salary: $120,000 The MemorialCare Long Beach Heart and Vascular Institute is offering a non-ACGME accredited Advanced Aortic Surgery Training program with an anticipated start date of Summer 2026. Employment through MemorialCare Medical Group will end by July 31, 2027. We will consider candidates who are board-eligible or board-certified by the American Board of Thoracic Surgery. The fellowship will have exposure to advanced and complex open, hybrid, and endovascular aortic surgery, TAVR and minimal-invasive atrial fibrillation procedures. Basic and advanced catheter and wire skills training will be offered during the year. Furthermore, there is an opportunity to participate in minimal-invasive heart valve surgery such as mini right thoracotomy AVR or MVR operations. MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center is one of the largest hospitals on the West Coast, with affiliations with the University of California Irvine and the University of California Los Angeles. The Comprehensive Aortic program includes ten vascular and cardiovascular surgeons enhanced by access to the best medical technology, a state-of-the-art hybrid room, and a dedicated patient transfer program. The faculty includes pioneers in aortic and cardiovascular surgery including Dr. White, Dr. Khoynezhad, Dr. Abolhoda, Dr. Chiu, and Dr. Sakwa. This fellowship is a well-structured, mentored program that will involve a diversified experience. In addition to the operations and procedures, the fellow will participate in a research program that encompasses industry-sponsored multi-center trials, physician-sponsored investigational device exemption, and outcomes clinical studies. A publication in peer-reviewed journal and presentation at a national meeting is sponsored and a requirement of the fellowship. For the cardiovascular surgeon this program will include: Ascending aortic and hemi-arch surgery Aortic root replacement surgery including David/Yacoub Concomitant MAZE procedures (concomitant and left thoracotomy) Minimal-invasive AF procedures (total thoracoscopic, sub-xiphoid, hybrid) Descending and thoracoabdominal aortic replacement Aortic arch reconstructions including hybrid Advanced TEVAR Branched arch and thoracoabdominal aortic TEVAR Percutaneous ascending aortic TEVAR EVAR TAVR Minimally invasive mitral, aortic, and tricuspid valve surgery
